## P8.159 - Reuse staged chunk expected metadata in planned-input readers

Status: done.

Goal: reduce repeated file-backed staged chunk scans in C5 planned worksheet
input paths while preserving the existing expected-size / expected-CRC
guardrails.

Input:
- P8.137 staged chunk expected-size metadata.
- P8.149 staged chunk expected-CRC metadata.
- `CurrentWorksheetInputChunkReader` planned staged-chunk paths used by
  follow-up cell replacement and bounded sheetData replacement.

Output:
- `PackageEntryChunkReader` now prefers already-recorded `PackageEntryChunk`
  `expected_size` / `expected_crc32` metadata instead of recomputing file-backed
  chunk size/CRC in every reader constructor.
- File-backed staged chunk mutation is still detected while the planned input is
  consumed: short reads, overrun reads, and same-size CRC changes keep the
  current worksheet input purpose, staged chunk index, file path, and
  expected/actual diagnostics.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` planned staged chunk mutation regressions now assert
  the read-boundary diagnostics for follow-up cell replacement and bounded
  sheetData replacement, plus unchanged no-state-pollution guarantees.

Acceptance:
- `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R "fastxlsx.package_editor.core"
  --output-on-failure` passes on the current VS2026/NMake workstation.

Forbidden:
- Do not weaken expected-size or expected-CRC enforcement.
- Do not claim cryptographic tamper resistance, relationship repair, broad
  preservation proof, or complete low-memory worksheet editing.

## P8.160 - Keep missing planned staged files on the read boundary

Status: done.

Goal: pin the P8.159 metadata-reuse behavior with a missing-file regression:
planned staged chunk readers must not re-stat or re-CRC already validated
file-backed chunks during construction, and a later missing chunk file should
surface at the consuming current-worksheet read boundary.

Input:
- P8.137 staged chunk expected-size metadata.
- P8.159 planned-input reader metadata reuse.
- Follow-up `PackageEditor::replace_worksheet_cells()` consumption of a
  previously queued planned staged worksheet.

Output:
- `PackageEntryChunkReader` read-time open failures now include the recorded
  expected byte count alongside the staged chunk index and file path.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` now deletes a validated planned staged file before
  a follow-up cell replacement and verifies the failure is reported as
  `failed to read current worksheet input for worksheet cell replacement
  analysis`, not as reader initialization.
- The regression also verifies unchanged `EditPlan` / calc policy / temp-file
  hygiene after failure, then restores the file and proves the same editor can
  complete the follow-up transform and save.

Acceptance:
- `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R "fastxlsx.package_editor.core"
  --output-on-failure` passes on the current VS2026/NMake workstation.

Forbidden:
- Do not reintroduce planned-input constructor scans for file-backed chunks that
  already carry expected metadata.
- Do not expose staged chunk metadata as public API or claim complete
  low-memory worksheet editing.

## P8.161 - Report expected size for missing save-time source-copy chunks

Status: done.

Goal: keep C5 save-time file-backed source-copy failures as actionable as
planned-input staged chunk failures when a PackageEditor-owned source-copy temp
file disappears after materialization but before package writing.

Input:
- P8.103 file-backed source-copy handoff for copy-original source entries.
- P8.147 source-copy expected-size metadata.
- `PackageEditorSourceCopyTempFilesHook` test seam used by source-copy temp
  mutation regressions.

Output:
- `PackageWriter` file-backed chunk stat/open failures now include the recorded
  expected byte count when `PackageEntryChunk::expected_size` is present.
- The stored bootstrap writer keeps the same expected-size context for
  read-time file-backed chunk open failures after preflight.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.policy` now deletes source-copy temp files before
  `write_package()` and verifies the failure keeps PackageEditor write context,
  ZIP entry/chunk/path context, `failed to stat file-backed ZIP entry chunk`,
  and expected-size diagnostics without overwriting output or mutating queued
  edit state; a later safe save still succeeds.

Acceptance:
- `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R "fastxlsx.package_editor.policy"
  --output-on-failure` passes on the current VS2026/NMake workstation.
- `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release-minizip -R "fastxlsx.package_editor.policy"
  --output-on-failure` passes on the current VS2026/NMake workstation.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose source-copy temp paths, hooks, or expected-size metadata as
  public API.
- Do not claim complete package streaming or complete low-memory worksheet
  editing.

## P8.162 - Allow chunked single-cell replacement payload backing

Status: done.

Goal: continue narrowing the C5 cell-replacement payload boundary by letting the
internal `WorksheetCellReplacementPayload` carry caller-owned multi-chunk XML,
while keeping the existing bounded preflight and output/audit chunk replay
semantics.

Input:
- P8.128 introduced the explicit replacement payload type.
- P8.132 made transformer emission and PackageEditor audit/output consume the
  payload through chunk replay.
- The remaining replacement-cell preflight still needs a bounded whole-cell
  view to validate the `<c>` root and matching unqualified `r` selector.

Output:
- `WorksheetCellReplacementPayload::from_chunks()` now records caller-owned
  `std::string_view` chunks and byte size; `from_materialized_xml()` is just the
  single-chunk convenience.
- Transformer preflight materializes only this bounded single-cell payload via
  `materialize_for_preflight()`, then validates the existing `<c>` / `r`
  contract.
- Transformer output and PackageEditor cell-replacement audit/output continue to
  replay payload chunks through `for_each_chunk()`.
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` covers multi-chunk replacement payload
  preflight plus replay, preserving caller chunk boundaries.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` covers an end-to-end by-name cell replacement
  using a multi-chunk replacement payload, including final XLSX output and temp
  cleanup.

Acceptance:
- `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R
  "fastxlsx\.(worksheet_transformer|package_editor\.core)$" --output-on-failure -j 1`
  passes on the current VS2026/NMake workstation.
- `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release-minizip -R
  "fastxlsx\.(worksheet_transformer|package_editor\.core)$" --output-on-failure -j 1`
  passes on the current VS2026/NMake workstation.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose `WorksheetCellReplacementPayload` as public API.
- Do not claim streamed replacement-cell payloads; preflight still materializes
  the bounded single-cell XML payload.
- Do not change worksheet source/planned input semantics, sharedStrings/styles
  migration, relationship repair, or complete low-memory worksheet editing.

## P8.163 - Guard chunked single-cell replacement payload size accounting

Status: done.

Goal: harden the remaining C5 single-cell replacement payload boundary after
P8.162 by making chunked payload byte-size accounting explicit and by keeping
the materialization guard attached to the preflight materialization call itself.

Input:
- P8.162 allowed `WorksheetCellReplacementPayload` to carry caller-owned
  multi-chunk single-cell XML.
- The remaining `<c>` payload preflight still materializes one bounded cell XML
  payload to validate root element and selector `r` attribute.
- The payload is internal and not a public streamed replacement source.

Output:
- `WorksheetCellReplacementPayload::from_chunks()` is now implemented out of
  line and rejects `std::size_t` byte-size overflow while recording caller
  chunks.
- `WorksheetCellReplacementPayload::materialize_for_preflight()` now enforces
  `worksheet_replacement_cell_xml_materialization_byte_limit` itself before
  reserving or appending bytes.
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` covers multi-chunk payloads whose aggregate
  size exceeds the single-cell materialization limit.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` covers by-name cell replacement with an
  oversized chunked payload and verifies the failure does not mutate Patch
  state or output preservation.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` passes.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` passes.
- Default and minizip targeted C5 shards pass when available.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose `WorksheetCellReplacementPayload` as public API.
- Do not claim replacement cell payloads are fully streamed; preflight still
  materializes one bounded single-cell XML payload.
- Do not change worksheet source/planned input semantics, sharedStrings/styles
  migration, relationship repair, or complete low-memory worksheet editing.

## P8.164 - Harden replacement cell payload scanner failures

Status: done.

Goal: harden the PackageEditor side of the remaining bounded replacement-cell
payload path after P8.163 by making the scanner retained-window capacity check
explicit and by preventing malformed chunked cell payload tails from silently
dropping payload audit.

Input:
- `WorksheetCellReplacementPayload` may now carry caller-owned multi-chunk
  single-cell XML, but preflight still materializes one bounded `<c>` payload
  for root / selector validation.
- The PackageEditor payload audit scanner replays those chunks to detect
  sharedString / style / formula payload dependencies.
- The scanner window is larger than the current single-cell materialization
  limit, so its size guard is defensive rather than a replacement for the
  single-cell payload guard.

Output:
- `scan_replacement_cell_payload_start_tags()` now uses an explicit
  retained-window capacity helper instead of inline `limit - buffer.size()`
  arithmetic.
- Scanner failures now propagate as `worksheet replacement cell XML payload
  scanner failed: ...` during `replace_worksheet_cells()` analysis, before any
  Patch state mutation.
- The following P8.165 structural preflight now catches malformed bounded cell
  payloads before the PackageEditor scanner has to diagnose those ordinary
  malformed XML cases.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` passes.
- Default and minizip targeted C5 shards pass when available.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose `WorksheetCellReplacementPayload` or the scanner as public API.
- Do not claim replacement cell payloads are fully streamed; preflight still
  materializes one bounded single-cell XML payload.
- Do not claim full cell schema validation, relationship repair, sharedStrings /
  styles migration, or complete low-memory worksheet editing.

## P8.165 - Validate bounded replacement cell XML structure in preflight

Status: done.

Goal: make the remaining bounded single-cell replacement payload safer before
PackageEditor analysis/output passes by rejecting malformed XML structure during
the existing transformer preflight materialization step.

Input:
- P8.163 / P8.164 already bound and replay `WorksheetCellReplacementPayload`
  chunks.
- Preflight still materializes one bounded replacement `<c>` payload for root
  and selector validation.
- Existing tests included an intentionally malformed replacement payload that
  used to force output-side relationship scanner parse-failure audit; after this
  slice that fixture must remain well-formed and use chunk-boundary splitting,
  with follow-up scanner behavior handled by P8.166.

Output:
- `validate_replacement_cell_xml_structure()` now verifies one bounded cell XML
  payload has a single `<c>` / `*:c` root, balanced local-name start/end tags,
  closed comments / CDATA / processing instructions, and no non-whitespace text
  outside the root.
- The check is local to the bounded materialized preflight payload and does not
  add worksheet DOM, full XML schema validation, namespace repair, or OpenXML
  cell schema validation.
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` covers unclosed root, mismatched nested tags,
  multiple roots, and chunked truncated child-tag payloads.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` covers chunked malformed replacement payloads
  failing before Patch state changes; the follow-up P8.166 slice turns the
  output-side relationship scanner split-tag fixture into a normal
  missing-relationship audit instead of a parse-failure audit.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` passes.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` passes.
- Default and minizip targeted C5 shards pass when available.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose the structural preflight as public API.
- Do not claim full XML parser, XML repair, OpenXML cell schema validation, or
  streamed replacement-cell payloads.
- Do not change worksheet source/planned input semantics, sharedStrings/styles
  migration, relationship repair, or complete low-memory worksheet editing.

## P8.166 - Make output relationship scanner chunk-aware

Status: done.

Goal: keep the output-side worksheet relationship-id audit usable when caller
replacement cell chunks split an otherwise valid XML tag or comment across chunk
boundaries.

Input:
- P8.117/P8.120 already fused relationship-id audit into output emission passes.
- P8.163/P8.165 made multi-chunk replacement cell payloads explicit and rejected
  malformed bounded cell XML during preflight.
- The remaining gap was `WorksheetRelationshipReferenceScanner::process()`
  treating every caller chunk as a complete XML fragment.

Output:
- `WorksheetRelationshipReferenceScanner` now keeps a bounded retained XML tail
  across `process()` calls, parses completed tags/comments after later chunks
  arrive, and exposes `finish()` so truncated final tags/comments still become
  audit parse failures.
- Cell-replacement and sheetData/worksheet output paths call `finish()` at the
  end of their output stream and keep scanner failures as relationship-id audit
  notes instead of aborting the rewrite.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` now covers a chunked replacement `<c>` payload
  whose `<drawing r:id="...">` tag is split across chunks: it no longer records
  `relationship-id audit could not parse`; it records the specific
  `MissingRelationships` structured audit for `rIdMissing`.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` passes.
- Default and minizip targeted C5 shards pass when available.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose the scanner as public API.
- Do not claim full XML parsing, schema validation, namespace repair, or
  relationship repair/pruning.
- Do not claim replacement cell payloads are fully streamed; preflight still
  materializes one bounded single-cell XML payload.

## P8.167 - Make output audit scanners CDATA-aware

Status: done.

Goal: keep output-side C5 audit scanners from treating relationship-like or
formula-like text inside CDATA as real worksheet markup when caller replacement
chunks split CDATA across chunk boundaries.

Input:
- P8.166 made the relationship-id scanner retain partial tags/comments across
  output chunks.
- Replacement cell XML structural preflight already accepts bounded CDATA
  inside the replacement `<c>` root.
- The remaining gap was that the relationship-id scanner and replacement-cell
  payload audit scanner could resume scanning inside CDATA text after a literal
  `>` before the closing `]]>`.

Output:
- `WorksheetRelationshipReferenceScanner` now recognizes `<![CDATA[` and skips
  through the matching `]]>` across retained output chunks; unfinished CDATA at
  stream end still becomes a relationship-id parse-failure audit.
- The replacement-cell payload start-tag scanner now has the same CDATA skip
  state, so `<f>`-like text inside CDATA does not become a formula dependency
  audit.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` now covers a multi-chunk replacement cell whose
  CDATA text contains `<drawing r:id="..."/>` and `<f>...</f>` literals; those
  literals do not create relationship or formula audits.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` passes.
- Default and minizip targeted C5 shards pass when available.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose these scanners as public API.
- Do not claim full XML parser, schema validation, namespace repair, CDATA
  normalization, relationship repair/pruning, or formula parsing.
- Do not claim replacement cell payloads are fully streamed; preflight still
  materializes one bounded single-cell XML payload.

## P8.168 - Make output audit scanners processing-instruction-aware

Status: done.

Goal: keep output-side C5 audit scanners from treating relationship-like or
formula-like text inside XML processing instructions as real worksheet markup
when caller replacement chunks split the processing instruction across chunk
boundaries.

Input:
- P8.166 made the relationship-id scanner chunk-aware for split tags/comments.
- P8.167 made the relationship-id scanner and replacement-cell payload audit
  scanner skip CDATA across chunks.
- Replacement cell XML structural preflight already accepts bounded processing
  instructions inside the replacement `<c>` root.
- The remaining gap was that both audit scanners could stop a processing
  instruction at the first literal `>` instead of the real `?>` terminator, then
  resume scanning relationship-like or formula-like PI text as markup.

Output:
- `WorksheetRelationshipReferenceScanner` now recognizes `<?...?>` and skips
  through the matching `?>` across retained output chunks; unfinished processing
  instructions at stream end still become relationship-id parse-failure audit.
- The replacement-cell payload start-tag scanner now has the same processing
  instruction skip state, so `<f>`-like text inside PI content does not become a
  formula dependency audit.
- The shared streaming tag-end helper now treats `<?...?>` as a PI boundary,
  avoiding first-`>` truncation for callers that route PI markup through that
  helper.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` now covers a multi-chunk replacement cell whose
  processing instruction text contains `<drawing r:id="..."/>` and `<f>...</f>`
  literals; those literals do not create relationship or formula audits.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` passes.
- Default and minizip targeted C5 shards pass when available.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose these scanners as public API.
- Do not claim full XML parser, schema validation, namespace repair, processing
  instruction normalization, relationship repair/pruning, or formula parsing.
- Do not claim replacement cell payloads are fully streamed; preflight still
  materializes one bounded single-cell XML payload.

## P8.169 - Make sheetData chunk-source start-tag scanner CDATA-aware

Status: done.

Goal: keep the direct `sheetData` replacement chunk-source scanner from treating
CDATA text inside replacement cells as real `sheetData` structure or payload
dependency markup.

Input:
- Direct/by-name `replace_worksheet_sheet_data_from_chunk_source()` already
  consumes caller replacement chunks once for root validation, payload dependency
  audit, relationship-id audit, and output insertion.
- P8.167/P8.168 hardened output-side relationship and replacement-cell payload
  audit scanners, but the `sheetData` start-tag scanner still treated `<![CDATA[`
  as generic `!` markup and could resume scanning CDATA text after a literal `>`.
- A replacement `<sheetData>` payload may contain inline-string CDATA inside a
  cell; that text must not be interpreted as `<c t="s">` or `<f>` markup.

Output:
- `scan_sheet_data_start_tags_from_chunk_source()` now has an `inside_cdata`
  state and skips through `]]>` across caller chunks.
- CDATA outside the `sheetData` root remains invalid and is rejected before Patch
  state changes.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata` covers a multi-chunk replacement `<sheetData>`
  payload whose CDATA text contains fake shared-string cell and formula markup;
  those literals do not create `SheetDataReplacement` sharedStrings or formula
  payload audits.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata` passes.
- Default and minizip targeted C5 shards pass when available.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose the scanner as public API.
- Do not claim full XML parser, schema validation, namespace repair, CDATA
  normalization, sharedStrings/style migration, relationship repair/pruning, or
  formula parsing.
- Do not claim replacement `<sheetData>` payloads are unbounded; caller chunks
  and retained scanner windows still follow the existing bounded chunk-source
  guardrails.

## P8.170 - Stream sheetData processing-instruction skip state

Status: done.

Goal: keep the direct `sheetData` replacement chunk-source scanner low-memory
when it skips XML processing instructions inside the replacement payload.

Input:
- P8.168 made generic tag-end detection treat `<?...?>` as processing
  instruction markup.
- P8.169 added CDATA skip state to the direct `sheetData` start-tag scanner.
- The remaining issue was that a long processing instruction inside replacement
  `<sheetData>` could still be retained as one pending tag until `?>`, tripping
  the event-window guard even when caller chunks were small.

Output:
- `scan_sheet_data_start_tags_from_chunk_source()` now has an
  `inside_processing_instruction` state and keeps only the terminator tail while
  waiting for `?>` across chunks.
- Unfinished processing instructions still fail before Patch state changes with
  a `sheetData replacement XML processing instruction is not closed` diagnostic.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata` covers a multi-chunk replacement
  `<sheetData>` payload with a long processing instruction body kept below the
  current `package_editor_sheet_data_replacement_payload_byte_limit`; fake
  `<c t="s">` / `<f>` literals inside the PI do not create
  `SheetDataReplacement` sharedStrings or formula audits.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata` passes.
- Default and minizip targeted C5 shards pass when available.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose the scanner as public API.
- Do not claim full XML parser, schema validation, namespace repair, processing
  instruction normalization, sharedStrings/style migration, relationship
  repair/pruning, or formula parsing.
- Do not claim replacement `<sheetData>` payloads are unbounded; individual
  caller chunks and other retained XML tokens still follow existing bounded
  chunk-source guardrails.

## P8.171 - Stream output relationship scanner ignored markup state

Status: done.

Goal: keep the output-side worksheet relationship-id scanner low-memory when
it skips long comments, CDATA sections, or XML processing instructions emitted
from sheetData / cell replacement chunks.

Input:
- P8.167/P8.168 made the relationship-id scanner skip CDATA and processing
  instruction text, but an unfinished ignored markup block could still retain
  the whole block until its terminator arrived.
- P8.170 fixed the direct sheetData start-tag scanner PI path; the output
  relationship-id scanner needed the same terminator-tail behavior.

Output:
- `WorksheetRelationshipReferenceScanner` now enters explicit ignored-markup
  states for comments, CDATA, and processing instructions, retaining only the
  closing-marker tail (`-->`, `]]>`, or `?>`) while waiting across chunks.
- Unclosed ignored markup still becomes a relationship-id parse-failure audit
  instead of being silently accepted.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata` covers long multi-chunk sheetData
  replacement CDATA and PI bodies kept below the current sheetData payload
  guard; relationship-like text inside them does not create relationship audits
  or parse-failure notes.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata` passes.
- Default and minizip targeted C5 shards pass when available.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose the scanner as public API.
- Do not claim full XML parser, schema validation, namespace repair, CDATA/PI
  normalization, relationship repair/pruning, streamed replacement-cell
  payloads, or complete large worksheet rewrite.
- Do not remove the retained-window guard for real tags or malformed markup.

## P8.172 - Stream replacement-cell payload ignored-markup chunks

Status: done.

Goal: keep the replacement-cell payload start-tag scanner bounded when caller
replacement chunks contain long comments, CDATA sections, or XML processing
instructions.

Input:
- P8.167/P8.168 made the replacement-cell payload scanner skip CDATA and
  processing instruction text for audit correctness.
- The scanner still checked a whole incoming payload chunk against
  `package_editor_cell_replacement_event_window_byte_limit` before processing
  it, so a single long ignored-markup chunk could fail before the scanner had a
  chance to trim the retained buffer to the close-marker tail.

Output:
- `scan_replacement_cell_payload_start_tags()` now appends caller payload chunks
  in bounded slices and processes each slice immediately, so comments, CDATA,
  and processing instructions can reduce retained state to the relevant
  terminator tail instead of requiring the whole incoming chunk to fit first.
- Real tags and malformed markup still use the existing retained-window guard;
  this change does not make replacement cell payloads unbounded or streamed
  beyond the current single-cell preflight model.
- A `FASTXLSX_ENABLE_TEST_HOOKS` package-editor scanner regression feeds one
  replacement-cell payload chunk larger than
  `package_editor_cell_replacement_event_window_byte_limit` for comment, CDATA,
  and processing-instruction cases; fake `<drawing r:id="...">` / `<f>` text
  inside ignored markup is skipped, and the scanner resumes on the real formula
  tag after the ignored block.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` passes in default and minizip presets.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose this as public API.
- Do not claim fully streamed replacement-cell payloads: normal
  `replace_worksheet_cells()` still preflights bounded single-cell XML under
  `worksheet_replacement_cell_xml_materialization_byte_limit`.
- Do not claim full XML parser, schema validation, namespace repair, CDATA/PI
  normalization, relationship repair/pruning, or complete large worksheet
  rewrite.

## P8.173 - Stream sheetData start-tag ignored-markup chunks

Status: done.

Goal: keep the sheetData replacement start-tag scanner bounded when caller
`<sheetData>` replacement chunks contain long comments, CDATA sections, or XML
processing instructions.

Input:
- The sheetData scanner already skipped comments, CDATA, and processing
  instructions while retaining only the close-marker tail once it entered the
  ignored-markup state.
- The outer chunk loop still checked a whole incoming caller chunk against the
  retained-window capacity before processing it, so a single long ignored block
  could fail before the scanner had a chance to shrink retained state.

Output:
- `scan_sheet_data_start_tags_from_chunk_source()` now appends caller chunks in
  bounded slices and processes each slice immediately, matching the
  replacement-cell payload scanner hardening from P8.172.
- A `FASTXLSX_ENABLE_TEST_HOOKS` package-editor scanner regression feeds one
  `<sheetData>` chunk larger than
  `package_editor_cell_replacement_event_window_byte_limit` for comment, CDATA,
  and processing-instruction cases; fake `<c t="s">` / `<f>` text inside ignored
  markup is skipped, and the scanner resumes on the real formula tag after the
  ignored block.
- The test is wired into `fastxlsx.package_editor.core`, so both default and
  minizip targeted package-editor core shards cover the guard.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` passes in default and minizip presets.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose this as public API.
- Do not claim sheetData payloads are unbounded: replacement `<sheetData>` still
  has the current bounded payload guard.
- Do not claim full XML parser, schema validation, namespace repair, CDATA/PI
  normalization, sharedStrings/styles migration, relationship repair/pruning, or
  complete large worksheet rewrite.

## P8.174 - Stream retained relationship tags before long chunks

Status: done.

Goal: keep the output-side worksheet relationship-id scanner bounded when a
caller chunk ends in the middle of a real XML tag and the next caller chunk is
large but closes that tag near the beginning.

Input:
- P8.166 made complete split tags parseable by retaining XML tails across caller
  chunks.
- P8.171 made long ignored markup low-memory once the scanner was already inside
  comment / CDATA / processing-instruction state.
- The remaining retained-tag path still appended the whole next caller chunk to
  the retained buffer before parsing, so a split real tag followed by a large
  ignored block could hit the retained-window guard before the scanner had a
  chance to parse the tag and enter ignored-markup tail mode.

Output:
- `WorksheetRelationshipReferenceScanner::process()` now feeds a retained
  partial tag with the next caller chunk in bounded slices. Once the retained
  tag is complete and parsed, the remaining caller chunk is processed normally,
  including ignored-markup tail retention.
- A `FASTXLSX_ENABLE_TEST_HOOKS` relationship scanner regression feeds
  `<dra` / `wing r:id="..."/>` split across chunks, followed by a single
  long comment chunk that exceeds
  `package_editor_cell_replacement_event_window_byte_limit`; the scanner records
  the real drawing relationship id and skips fake relationship/formula text in
  the following comment.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` passes in default and minizip presets.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose this as public API.
- Do not claim relationship repair/pruning, full XML parser behavior, schema
  validation, namespace repair, unbounded XML tags, or complete large worksheet
  rewrite.
- Do not bypass the existing retained-window guard for genuinely long
  unfinished real tags.

## P8.175 - Enforce staged memory chunk expected-size on replay

Status: done.

Goal: keep internal staged package-entry chunk metadata consistent for
memory-backed chunks, not only file-backed chunks.

Input:
- P8.137 introduced staged chunk expected-size metadata.
- P8.158 added expected-CRC metadata for staged chunks.
- P8.173/P8.174 continued C5 chunk-source hardening around retained scanner
  windows, but `PackageEntryChunkReader` still allowed a memory chunk with stale
  expected-size metadata to replay successfully when no expected-CRC metadata was
  recorded.

Output:
- `PackageEntryChunkReader` now validates memory-backed chunks against recorded
  `expected_size` during replay, including empty memory chunks.
- The failure message names the staged package-entry chunk index, marks it as a
  memory chunk, and reports expected/actual byte counts.
- A `FASTXLSX_ENABLE_TEST_HOOKS` helper replays staged package-entry chunks to a
  string so the focused regression can exercise the reader boundary directly.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` covers the stale memory expected-size path where
  `has_expected_crc32 == false`, proving size metadata is enforced independently
  of CRC metadata.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` passes in default and minizip presets.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose `PackageEntryChunk` expected-size metadata or replay hooks as
  public API.
- Do not describe this as a new package writer feature; it is internal staged
  chunk state hygiene.
- Do not claim this changes file-backed chunk semantics, PackageReader
  materialization, relationship repair/pruning, or complete low-memory worksheet
  editing.

## P8.176 - Enforce empty staged memory chunk expected-CRC on replay

Status: done.

Goal: make memory-backed staged chunk CRC validation apply equally to empty
chunks, even though empty chunks do not emit an output payload chunk.

Input:
- P8.158 added expected-CRC metadata for staged chunks.
- P8.175 made memory-backed staged chunks enforce expected-size during
  `PackageEntryChunkReader` replay.
- The empty memory chunk branch still continued before CRC validation, so stale
  expected-CRC metadata on an empty chunk could be skipped.

Output:
- `PackageEntryChunkReader` now validates memory chunk expected-size and
  expected-CRC before deciding whether an empty memory chunk should be skipped or
  a non-empty memory chunk should be emitted.
- A focused `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` regression uses the internal
  package-entry chunk replay hook to verify an empty memory chunk with stale
  `expected_crc32` fails with staged chunk context and expected/actual CRC
  diagnostics.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` passes in default and minizip presets.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose the replay hook or expected-CRC metadata as public API.
- Do not describe this as cryptographic tamper resistance; it is internal
  consistency hygiene for staged chunks.
- Do not claim this changes file-backed chunk semantics, ZIP reading, worksheet
  relationship repair, or complete low-memory worksheet editing.

## P8.177 - Preserve self-closing pass-through tags once in transformer output

Status: done.

Goal: keep worksheet transformer output byte semantics correct when the event
reader represents self-closing structural tags as a start event plus a synthetic
end event.

Input:
- The event reader emits synthetic end events for self-closing worksheet,
  sheetData, row, and cell tags, carrying the same `raw_xml` as the start event
  and `self_closing=true`.
- The transformer output emitter previously wrote every pass-through action's
  `raw_xml`, so non-target self-closing cells/rows/sheetData could be duplicated
  in rewritten worksheet output.

Output:
- `emit_cell_replacement_worksheet_from_chunk_source()` now skips pass-through
  synthetic self-closing end events for worksheet, sheetData, row, and cell
  boundaries while still emitting the original self-closing start event once.
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` covers pass-through `<c/>`, `<row/>`, and
  `<sheetData/>` output, including a replacement in the same worksheet and a
  missing-selector diagnostic case.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` was rerun in default and minizip presets because
  PackageEditor cell replacement uses this transformer output path.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` passes in the default preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` passes in default and minizip presets.

Forbidden:
- Do not change event-reader event semantics or remove synthetic end events from
  scan/action callbacks.
- Do not claim XML schema validation, namespace repair, relationship repair, or
  complete large worksheet editing.
- Do not treat this as a public API change.

## P8.178 - Label unknown staged chunk kind in PackageEntryChunkReader diagnostics

Status: done.

Goal: keep C5 staged package-entry chunk replay failures actionable when an
invalid internal `PackageEntryChunk::kind` reaches `PackageEntryChunkReader`.

Input:
- Writer-side chunk diagnostics already identify unknown chunk kinds as
  `(unknown)`.
- `PackageEntryChunkReader` used the staged chunk index but did not label the
  unknown kind in its replay/preflight context.

Output:
- `staged_package_entry_chunk_context()` now emits `(unknown)` for unsupported
  `PackageEntryChunk::kind` values.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` covers the internal replay hook with an
  unsupported chunk kind and verifies the error carries both the chunk index /
  `(unknown)` kind and the original unsupported-kind detail.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` passes in the default preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` passes in the minizip preset.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose `PackageEntryChunk` or replay hooks as public API.
- Do not introduce a recovery path for unknown chunk kinds.
- Do not claim this changes package writer behavior, ZIP reading, relationship
  repair, or complete low-memory worksheet editing.

## P8.179 - Classify xml-stylesheet as processing instruction

Status: done.

Goal: keep the pull-based worksheet event reader's token categories accurate
for XML processing instructions whose target starts with `xml` but is not the
XML declaration.

Input:
- `emit_processing_instruction()` previously treated any raw token starting with
  `<?xml` as `WorksheetEventKind::XmlDeclaration`.
- `<?xml-stylesheet ...?>` should remain a processing instruction while keeping
  raw pass-through bytes unchanged.

Output:
- The event reader now classifies only `<?xml` followed by whitespace or `?>`
  as `XmlDeclaration`; `<?xml-stylesheet ...?>` is emitted as
  `ProcessingInstruction`.
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_event_reader` covers the distinction through a chunked
  source so the classification is verified after token reassembly.
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` was rerun as the adjacent consumer of the
  event stream.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_event_reader` passes in the default preset.
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` passes in the default preset.

Forbidden:
- Do not add XML schema validation, namespace repair, or stylesheet semantics.
- Do not change pass-through raw XML bytes.
- Do not expose worksheet event reader APIs publicly.

## P8.180 - Reject XML declaration after worksheet root start

Status: done.

Goal: keep the pull-based worksheet event reader from passing invalid XML
declaration tokens through the transformer / PackageEditor rewrite paths after
the worksheet root has started.

Input:
- P8.179 narrowed XML declaration classification to true `<?xml ...?>` tokens.
- The reader still allowed a true XML declaration token inside the worksheet
  root because processing-instruction handling only rejected markup after the
  root ended.

Output:
- `emit_processing_instruction()` now rejects a true XML declaration after
  `WorksheetStart` with a root-position diagnostic.
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_event_reader` covers the rejected late XML declaration
  through a chunked source, and also verifies ordinary processing instructions
  inside the root remain pass-through tokens.
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` and `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` were
  rerun because both consume the event-reader stream; `fastxlsx.package_editor.core`
  was also rerun under the minizip preset.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_event_reader` passes in the default preset.
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` passes in the default preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` passes in default and minizip presets.

Forbidden:
- Do not reject ordinary processing instructions inside the worksheet root.
- Do not add XML schema validation, namespace repair, or XML repair.
- Do not expose worksheet event reader APIs publicly.

## P8.181 - Validate worksheet cell value wrapper boundaries

Status: done.

Goal: keep the pull-based worksheet event reader from silently passing malformed
cell value wrapper markup through C5 transformer / PackageEditor rewrite paths.

Input:
- P8.180 hardened XML declaration handling after the worksheet root starts.
- The reader still treated any closing `v` / `t` / `f` tag inside a cell as a
  valid cell value boundary, even when it did not match the opened value tag or
  appeared without a corresponding start tag.

Output:
- `WorksheetEventState` now tracks the current open cell value wrapper local
  name and rejects mismatched closing tags, orphan value closing tags, nested
  value wrappers, and a closing cell boundary while a value wrapper is still
  open.
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_event_reader` covers mismatched `<v>...</f>`, orphan
  `</v>`, and nested `<v><t>...</t></v>` malformed source cases through chunked
  sources.
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` covers propagation of the event-reader
  mismatch diagnostic through the chunk-source emitter path.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` was rerun because PackageEditor consumes the
  same event-reader stream; the shard was also rerun under the minizip preset.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_event_reader` passes in the default preset.
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` passes in the default preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` passes in default and minizip presets.

Forbidden:
- Do not add full XML element-stack validation or XML schema validation.
- Do not change pass-through raw XML bytes for valid worksheets.
- Do not expose worksheet event reader APIs publicly.

## P8.182 - Guard worksheet core element nesting in event reader

Status: done.

Goal: keep the pull-based worksheet event reader from silently accepting invalid
core worksheet nesting that would make C5 transformer / PackageEditor rewrites
ambiguous.

Input:
- P8.181 added cell value wrapper boundary validation.
- The reader still accepted duplicate `sheetData` starts and nested `row` /
  `c` starts until a later closing boundary happened to fail or the stream ended.

Output:
- `WorksheetEventState` now records whether `sheetData` has already appeared,
  rejects duplicate or nested `sheetData`, rejects a `row` start while another
  row/cell is open, and rejects a `c` start while another cell is open.
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_event_reader` covers duplicate `<sheetData/>`, nested
  row, and nested cell malformed source cases through chunked sources.
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` covers propagation of a nested-cell
  event-reader diagnostic through the chunk-source emitter path.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` was rerun because PackageEditor consumes the
  same event-reader stream; the shard was also rerun under the minizip preset.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_event_reader` passes in the default preset.
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` passes in the default preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` passes in default and minizip presets.

Forbidden:
- Do not add full arbitrary XML stack validation or worksheet schema
  validation.
- Do not require a `sheetData` element for otherwise empty worksheet roots.
- Do not expose worksheet event reader APIs publicly.

## P8.183 - Contextualize PackageEditor malformed worksheet event failures

Status: done.

Goal: keep C5 PackageEditor cell replacement failures actionable when the
current worksheet input is readable but malformed at the event-reader /
transformer boundary, while preserving no-state-pollution guarantees.

Input:
- P8.181/P8.182 added event-reader rejection for mismatched cell value wrappers
  and invalid core worksheet nesting.
- The transformer propagated those diagnostics, but `PackageEditor` cell
  replacement analysis did not add the higher-level current-worksheet-input
  boundary when the scanner failed on malformed worksheet XML rather than on
  ZIP/staged-chunk IO.

Output:
- `PackageEditor::replace_worksheet_cells()` now wraps the transformer
  analysis pass with the `current worksheet input for worksheet cell
  replacement analysis` boundary and preserves the underlying event-reader
  diagnostic.
- The cell replacement output pass is wrapped similarly with the `current
  worksheet input for worksheet cell replacement output` boundary.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` now covers malformed source worksheet events
  for mismatched `<v>...</f>` and nested `<c>` inputs, verifies the analysis
  boundary plus event-reader diagnostic, and asserts failure does not mutate
  `EditPlan`, package-entry audits, worksheet payload / relationship audits,
  calc policy, manifest write mode, or temporary files.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` passes in the default preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` passes in the minizip preset.
- Adjacent `fastxlsx.worksheet_event_reader` and `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer`
  remain green in the default preset.

Forbidden:
- Do not add XML repair, schema validation, namespace repair, or relationship
  repair.
- Do not materialize the whole worksheet to improve diagnostics.
- Do not expose PackageEditor or worksheet transformer APIs publicly.

## P8.184 - Split sheetData source-input and replacement-payload diagnostics

Status: done.

Goal: keep C5 sheetData rewrite diagnostics precise now that one output writer
consumes both the current worksheet input and caller-provided replacement
`<sheetData>` chunks.

Input:
- P8.183 added PackageEditor context for malformed source worksheet events in
  cell replacement.
- `replace_worksheet_sheet_data_from_chunk_source()` already used
  `CurrentWorksheetInputChunkReader` for source ZIP/staged-chunk read failures
  and separately wrapped replacement chunk-source read failures, but malformed
  source worksheet event-reader failures still surfaced without the sheetData
  current-input output boundary.
- A naive wrapper around the whole output writer would incorrectly label
  replacement `<sheetData>` payload failures as current worksheet input failures.

Output:
- `write_worksheet_sheet_data_replacement_stream_from_chunk_source()` now wraps
  non-replacement event-scan failures with the `current worksheet input for
  worksheet sheetData replacement output` boundary.
- Replacement `<sheetData>` chunk-source read / validation failures keep the
  replacement-payload boundary and are not mislabeled as current worksheet input.
- Existing no-state-pollution sheetData tests now assert malformed source
  worksheet XML reports the current worksheet input output boundary while
  replacement `<sheetData>` errors do not contain that source-input boundary.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata` passes in the default preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata` passes in the minizip preset.
- Adjacent `fastxlsx.package_editor.core`, `fastxlsx.worksheet_event_reader`,
  and `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` remain green in the default preset.

Forbidden:
- Do not add XML repair, schema validation, namespace repair, or relationship
  repair.
- Do not collapse source worksheet input diagnostics and replacement payload
  diagnostics into one broad wrapper.
- Do not materialize the whole worksheet to improve diagnostics.
- Do not expose PackageEditor or worksheet transformer APIs publicly.

## P8.185 - Contextualize missing source sheetData as current worksheet input

Status: done.

Goal: keep the C5 sheetData output boundary precise when the current worksheet
is well-formed enough to scan but contains no `<sheetData>` element.

Input:
- P8.184 split malformed source worksheet event-reader failures from
  replacement `<sheetData>` payload failures.
- The no-`sheetData` source worksheet path still failed after the source scan
  without the sheetData current-input output boundary.

Output:
- `write_worksheet_sheet_data_replacement_stream_from_chunk_source()` now reuses
  the same current worksheet input output context for both event-scan failures
  and missing source `<sheetData>` diagnostics.
- Replacement payload errors still keep their replacement-payload context and
  are not relabeled as source worksheet input.
- Existing no-state-pollution sheetData tests now assert the missing source
  `<sheetData>` error names the current worksheet input boundary and does not
  contain replacement-payload wording.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata` passes in the default preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata` passes in the minizip preset.
- Adjacent default C5 shards remain green when available.

Forbidden:
- Do not add XML repair, synthetic `<sheetData>`, schema validation,
  namespace repair, relationship repair, or public editor API.
- Do not consume caller replacement chunks when the source worksheet has no
  `<sheetData>` target.

## P8.186 - Consolidate sheetData current-input output diagnostics

Status: done.

Goal: keep the C5 sheetData current-input output boundary consistent across
the chunk reader purpose, event-scan wrapper, missing-target diagnostics, and
pre-`sheetData` malformed source failures.

Input:
- P8.184/P8.185 gave source worksheet event-scan and missing `<sheetData>`
  failures the same sheetData current-input output boundary.
- The same wording still appeared in multiple PackageEditor call sites, making
  future diagnostic drift easy.
- A source worksheet whose root is not `<worksheet>` should fail before caller
  replacement `<sheetData>` chunks are consumed.

Output:
- Added one internal
  `worksheet_sheet_data_replacement_output_input_context` string_view and reused
  it for `CurrentWorksheetInputChunkReader` and sheetData output writer
  diagnostics.
- Added a small helper for `failed to consume <purpose>: <detail>` current-input
  messages.
- Extended sheetData no-state-pollution coverage with an invalid source root
  case that asserts the current-input output boundary, no replacement-payload
  wording, zero caller replacement chunk reads, no edit-plan note mutation, no
  temp leak, and source package preservation on subsequent `save_as()`.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata` passes in the default preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata` passes in the minizip preset.
- Adjacent default C5 shards remain green when available.

Forbidden:
- Do not add XML repair, synthetic worksheet/sheetData generation, schema
  validation, namespace repair, relationship repair, or public editor API.
- Do not consume caller replacement chunks for source worksheet root failures
  that happen before the first `<sheetData>` event.

## P8.187 - Extend by-name sheetData current-input invalid-root coverage

Status: done.

Goal: keep the C5 by-name `sheetData` chunk-source path aligned with the direct
part path for current worksheet input output diagnostics, while reducing future
diagnostic drift in the adjacent cell-replacement contexts.

Input:
- P8.186 consolidated the direct sheetData current-input output wording and
  added direct invalid-source-root coverage.
- The by-name helper delegates to the same internal `sheetData` replacement
  path after resolving the worksheet part through the workbook catalog, but the
  invalid-source-root no-state-pollution assertion only covered direct part
  replacement.
- Cell replacement analysis/output current-input context strings were still
  embedded as literals at the PackageEditor call sites.

Output:
- Added internal PackageEditor constants for cell replacement analysis/output
  current worksheet input context strings, matching the existing sheetData
  current-input output constant pattern.
- The cell replacement analyze/write catch wrappers now reuse the same
  current-worksheet-input failure-message helper, preserving existing wording
  while removing another pair of hard-coded context strings.
- Extended `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata` with a by-name invalid source
  worksheet root case. The test resolves `"Sheet1"` through the workbook
  catalog, then proves the source-root failure names `current worksheet input
  for worksheet sheetData replacement output`, does not mention replacement
  payload wording, reads zero caller replacement chunks, leaves edit plan,
  package-entry audits, relationship audits, calc policy, manifest write mode
  and temp files unchanged, and still preserves the source package on
  subsequent `save_as()`.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata` passes in the default preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata` passes in the minizip preset when
  available.
- Adjacent default C5 shards remain green when available.

Forbidden:
- Do not add XML repair, synthetic worksheet/sheetData generation, schema
  validation, namespace repair, relationship repair, or public editor API.
- Do not consume caller replacement chunks for by-name source worksheet root
  failures that happen after catalog resolution but before the first
  `<sheetData>` event.

## P8.188 - Cover by-name sheetData source-entry read failure parity

Status: done.

Goal: keep the C5 by-name `sheetData` helper aligned with the direct part path
when the resolved current worksheet input is a corrupt source package entry.

Input:
- P8.138/P8.184 already made direct sheetData source-entry ZIP read / CRC
  failures name the `current worksheet input for worksheet sheetData
  replacement output` boundary while preserving the lower-level entry
  diagnostics.
- P8.187 extended by-name coverage for malformed source XML after workbook
  catalog target resolution, but by-name source-entry CRC/read failure parity
  was not asserted.

Output:
- Extended `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` with a by-name sheetData source-entry
  CRC failure case. The test opens the package, corrupts only the source
  worksheet bytes, resolves `"Sheet1"` through the workbook catalog, and then
  proves the failure reports the sheetData current-input output boundary,
  preserves `ZIP entry 'xl/worksheets/sheet1.xml' CRC mismatch` plus expected /
  actual CRC diagnostics, does not use replacement-payload wording, and leaves
  edit-plan parts, notes, package-entry audits, worksheet payload/reference
  audits, calc policy, manifest write mode, and PackageEditor temp files
  unchanged.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` passes in the default preset.
- Adjacent `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata`,
  `fastxlsx.worksheet_event_reader`, and `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer`
  shards remain green in the default preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata` still passes in the minizip preset when
  available.

Forbidden:
- Do not add XML repair, ZIP repair, source package mutation, relationship
  repair, or public editor API.
- Do not relabel source-entry read/CRC failures as replacement `<sheetData>`
  payload failures.

## P8.189 - Cover WorkbookEditor facade current-input read diagnostics

Status: done.

Goal: prove the public `WorkbookEditor::replace_sheet_data()` facade preserves
the C5 by-name `sheetData` current-input diagnostics and public state hygiene
when the already-open source worksheet entry becomes unreadable before the
replacement call.

Input:
- P8.188 covered the internal by-name `PackageEditor` path for corrupt source
  worksheet entry reads after workbook-catalog resolution.
- `WorkbookEditor::replace_sheet_data()` delegates through the same internal
  by-name chunk-source helper, but the public facade had no focused regression
  proving it does not swallow, relabel, or commit state after that internal
  failure.

Output:
- Extended `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` with a public-facade source-read failure
  case. The test opens a writer-generated two-sheet workbook, corrupts the
  target worksheet entry after open, calls `replace_sheet_data("Data", ...)`,
  and proves the thrown `FastXlsxError` still carries the `current worksheet
  input for worksheet sheetData replacement output` boundary and is not
  mislabeled as `sheetData replacement XML`.
- In the default stored ZIP preset, the same regression also asserts the
  lower-level `xl/worksheets/sheet1.xml` entry context and expected / actual CRC
  diagnostics. In the minizip preset, the test corrupts the target entry's
  central CRC metadata rather than compressed payload bytes so the failure stays
  at the source-entry read/metadata boundary instead of degrading into malformed
  XML after decompression.
- The regression verifies `has_pending_changes()`, `pending_change_count()`,
  `pending_replacement_cell_count()`, and
  `estimated_pending_replacement_memory_usage()` remain clean after the failure,
  the opened sheet catalog is still queryable, and the same editor can
  successfully replace and save after the source artifact is restored.
- `tests/test_workbook_editor.cpp` now prints the missing substring and failing
  diagnostic text for `check_contains()` failures to keep future facade
  diagnostic drift actionable.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes in the default preset.
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes in the minizip preset when available.
- Adjacent `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata`,
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.core`, `fastxlsx.worksheet_event_reader`, and
  `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` default shards remain green when run
  individually or within their CTest timeout budget.

Forbidden:
- Do not add public `PackageEditor`, `WorksheetEditor`, random cell editing,
  source ZIP repair, XML repair, relationship repair, or sharedStrings/styles
  migration.
- Do not claim this proves fully streamed replacement-cell payloads or complete
  low-memory large-file editing.

## P8.190 - Cover WorkbookEditor facade source XML diagnostics parity

Status: done.

Goal: prove the public `WorkbookEditor::replace_sheet_data()` facade also
preserves the C5 `sheetData` current-input diagnostics and public state hygiene
when the source worksheet entry is readable but its XML is not a valid
`sheetData` replacement target.

Input:
- P8.184/P8.185/P8.186 covered internal malformed source worksheet events,
  missing source `<sheetData>`, and consolidated current-input wording.
- P8.187/P8.188 extended the same internal behavior through the by-name helper
  after workbook-catalog resolution.
- P8.189 locked public facade behavior for source-entry read/CRC failures, but
  public `WorkbookEditor` still lacked focused coverage for readable source XML
  that fails during the sheetData output scan.

Output:
- Extended `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` with public facade coverage for a
  source worksheet that opens successfully but has no `<sheetData>` element.
  `replace_sheet_data("Data", ...)` fails with
  `current worksheet input for worksheet sheetData replacement output`, explains
  `worksheet XML sheetData element is missing`, and is not mislabeled as
  `sheetData replacement XML`.
- Added a second public facade case for malformed source `sheetData` XML
  where the event reader reports an invalid worksheet boundary. The error keeps
  the same sheetData current-input output boundary and preserves the
  event-reader diagnostic rather than blaming the caller replacement payload.
- Both cases verify `has_pending_changes()`, `pending_change_count()`,
  `pending_replacement_cell_count()`, and
  `estimated_pending_replacement_memory_usage()` remain clean after the failed
  replacement, the opened sheet catalog remains queryable, and the same editor
  can still apply a valid catalog-only `rename_sheet()` and `save_as()`.
- The test rewrites the writer-generated source package as stored ZIP entries
  after replacing only `xl/worksheets/sheet1.xml`, so the regression remains
  backend-neutral across default and minizip presets without touching internal
  `PackageEditor` APIs.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes in the default preset.
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes in the minizip preset when available.
- `git diff --check` stays clean apart from pre-existing line-ending warnings.

Forbidden:
- Do not add public `PackageEditor`, `WorksheetEditor`, random cell editing,
  source ZIP repair, XML repair, synthetic `<sheetData>` generation,
  relationship repair, or sharedStrings/styles migration.
- Do not claim this proves public large-file low-memory editing, PackageReader
  input-side streaming, or full worksheet XML validation.

## P8.191 - Preserve WorkbookEditor planned-catalog replacement diagnostics

Status: done.

Goal: keep the public `WorkbookEditor` facade consistent when a queued
`rename_sheet()` changes the planned workbook catalog before a follow-up
`replace_sheet_data()` call.

Input:
- Internal by-name worksheet and `sheetData` helpers resolve through the current
  planned workbook catalog after a workbook metadata rewrite, so the old source
  sheet name fails and the new planned name succeeds.
- Public `worksheet_names()` and `has_worksheet()` intentionally remain
  source-workbook inspection helpers inside the open session.
- `WorkbookEditor` tracked pending replacement diagnostics by caller sheet name;
  a replacement queued before rename and another replacement queued after rename
  could double-count stale replacement cells in
  `pending_replacement_cell_count()`.

Output:
- `WorkbookEditor::rename_sheet()` now migrates the public pending replacement
  diagnostic from the old sheet name to the new planned sheet name only after the
  internal rename succeeds. Failed renames still leave diagnostics unchanged.
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` now covers a planned-catalog public facade flow:
  after `rename_sheet("Data", "RenamedOnly")`, a follow-up
  `replace_sheet_data("Data", ...)` fails without adding public pending changes,
  replacement cells, or replacement memory, and `save_as()` still preserves the
  queued rename plus source sheet data.
- The same regression covers `replace_sheet_data("Data", ...)` followed by
  `rename_sheet("Data", "RenamedData")`: the pending replacement diagnostic is
  migrated, an old-name follow-up replacement fails without state pollution, and
  `replace_sheet_data("RenamedData", ...)` overwrites the stale pre-rename
  payload so saved output contains the renamed catalog and only the final cells.
- The public header now documents that follow-up `replace_sheet_data()` lookup
  uses the current planned catalog after a queued rename; `worksheet_names()` /
  `has_worksheet()` report that same planned catalog, while
  `source_worksheet_names()` / `has_source_worksheet()` keep the opened source
  workbook view visible.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes in the default preset.
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes in the minizip preset when available.
- `git diff --check` stays clean apart from pre-existing line-ending warnings.

Forbidden:
- Do not add public `PackageEditor`, `WorksheetEditor`, random cell editing,
  sheet add/delete, semantic sheet rename, relationship repair, sharedStrings /
  styles migration, or new public diagnostics.
- Do not claim this completes public large-file low-memory editing or full
  planned workbook catalog mutation support.

## P8.192 - Preserve WorkbookEditor diagnostics after failed rename

Status: done.

Goal: keep the public `WorkbookEditor` facade state-clean when `rename_sheet()`
fails after a replacement payload has already been queued.

Input:
- P8.191 moved pending replacement diagnostics across a successful
  `rename_sheet()` so follow-up replacement by planned name does not
  double-count stale payloads.
- The adjacent failure path still needed a public facade regression proving that
  duplicate-name or invalid-name rename failures do not migrate diagnostics,
  increment public pending-change counts, or disturb the old-name replacement
  payload.

Output:
- `WorkbookEditor::rename_sheet()` now routes diagnostic migration through a
  small internal helper. The migration still runs only after the internal
  planned-catalog rename succeeds, and it keeps one final diagnostic entry if a
  stale target-name entry is ever present.
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` now covers replacement-then-failed-rename state
  hygiene: after `replace_sheet_data("Data", ...)`, duplicate and invalid
  `rename_sheet()` calls leave `pending_change_count()`,
  `pending_replacement_cell_count()`, and
  `estimated_pending_replacement_memory_usage()` unchanged.
- The same coverage now also pins the public `last_edit_error()` behavior for
  those failures: duplicate and invalid rename failures record the rejected
  source/target names, a later invalid-name failure replaces the prior duplicate
  diagnostic, and both failures preserve `worksheet_catalog()` /
  `pending_worksheet_edits()` summaries.
- The same regression verifies a later valid `replace_sheet_data("Data", ...)`
  still overwrites the stale pre-failure payload and `save_as()` keeps the
  original source catalog name with only the final replacement cells.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes in the default preset.
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes in the minizip preset when available.
- `git diff --check` stays clean apart from pre-existing line-ending warnings.

Forbidden:
- Do not add public `PackageEditor`, `WorksheetEditor`, random cell editing,
  semantic sheet rename, relationship repair, sharedStrings / styles migration,
  or new public diagnostics.
- Do not claim this completes public large-file low-memory editing or full
  planned workbook catalog mutation support.

## P8.193 - Contextualize current worksheet input source diagnostics

Status: done.

Goal: keep C5 current-worksheet-input failures actionable by naming the concrete
input source used by `PackageEditor` cell replacement and sheetData rewrite
passes.

Input:
- P8.183-P8.188 already split current worksheet input failures from replacement
  payload failures and covered source-entry CRC/read failures.
- `CurrentWorksheetInputChunkReader` was already the single reader adapter for
  source worksheet entries and planned staged worksheet chunks, but its read
  diagnostics named only the pass boundary, not the concrete input source.

Output:
- `CurrentWorksheetInputChunkReader` now appends a source description to
  initialization and read failures: source worksheet entries are reported as
  `source worksheet entry 'xl/worksheets/sheetN.xml'`, while planned inputs are
  reported as planned worksheet staged chunks for the same worksheet entry.
- Existing source-read failure regressions now assert this source description
  for direct cell replacement, direct sheetData replacement, and by-name
  sheetData replacement. Adjacent planned staged-chunk size/CRC/missing-file
  regressions also assert the planned staged-chunk source description. The same
  failures still preserve the current worksheet input pass boundary, lower-level
  ZIP entry / CRC or staged-chunk diagnostics, and no-state-pollution checks.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` passes in the default preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata` and `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` pass in
  the default preset.
- The same three focused shards pass in the minizip preset when available.

Forbidden:
- Do not materialize full worksheet XML to improve diagnostics.
- Do not change public `WorkbookEditor` / `PackageReader` APIs or make internal
  `PackageEditor` public.
- Do not claim this completes low-memory large-file editing, relationship
  repair/pruning, sharedStrings/styles migration, or streamed replacement-cell
  payloads.

## P8.194 - Contextualize worksheet target preflight diagnostics and split C5 shard

Status: done.

Goal: keep C5 preflight failures as actionable as current-input read failures
when the requested worksheet target or source worksheet entry is absent before
any chunk-source reader can run.

Input:
- P8.193 added concrete source descriptions to `CurrentWorksheetInputChunkReader`
  initialization/read failures.
- Missing worksheet target / missing source-entry preflight failures still named
  the operation, but did not include the requested worksheet part or ZIP entry.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` had accumulated enough C5 coverage to risk the
  60s CTest timeout after adding more current-input diagnostics.

Output:
- Worksheet replacement, `sheetData` replacement, cell replacement, and the
  internal prevalidated worksheet staged-commit path now report both
  `worksheet part '/xl/worksheets/sheetN.xml'` and
  `ZIP entry 'xl/worksheets/sheetN.xml'` in target-preflight diagnostics.
- `require_current_worksheet_input_source()` also includes the concrete source
  worksheet entry when rejecting package-entry string replacements,
  materialized string replacements, or missing source entries.
- Added focused no-state-pollution coverage for cell replacement against a
  package whose workbook catalog references a worksheet entry that is absent.
  Existing worksheet and `sheetData` missing-target tests now assert the same
  part/entry context.
- Added `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` and moved C5 current-input /
  cell-replacement / planned staged-chunk diagnostics from the monolithic core
  shard into that 60s shard.

Acceptance:
- Default preset:
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.(core|c5|sheetdata)` passes.
- Minizip preset:
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.(core|c5|sheetdata)` passes.

Forbidden:
- Do not create missing worksheet parts, synthesize `<sheetData>`, or repair the
  package catalog.
- Do not expand public API or expose internal `PackageEditor`.
- Do not relax staged chunk CRC/size contracts or the 60s shard discipline.
- Do not claim this completes low-memory large-file editing, relationship
  repair/pruning, sharedStrings/styles migration, or streamed replacement-cell
  payloads.

## P8.196 - Add planned sheet-name context to by-name current-input failures

Status: done.

Goal: make C5 by-name worksheet diagnostics preserve the planned workbook
catalog sheet name after target resolution, without weakening the lower-level
source-entry / staged-chunk current-input boundary.

Input:
- P8.195 made direct and by-name source-read failures report source worksheet
  entry path, worksheet part, ZIP entry, and ZIP CRC details.
- When a planned workbook catalog renamed a sheet, the later by-name sheetData
  source-read failure still had part/entry detail, but did not explicitly carry
  the requested planned sheet name in the PackageEditor-level context.

Output:
- `PackageEditor::replace_worksheet_sheet_data_from_chunk_source_by_name()`
  and `PackageEditor::replace_worksheet_cells_by_name()` now wrap failures
  after target resolution with `by-name ... for sheet '<name>' resolved to
  worksheet part ...` context.
- C5 regression now covers a planned workbook catalog rename where
  `replace_worksheet_sheet_data_from_chunk_source_by_name("Renamed", ...)`
  resolves the planned sheet name to the original worksheet part, then fails
  on a corrupt source worksheet entry. The error keeps the planned sheet name,
  resolved worksheet part, ZIP entry, sheetData current-input output boundary,
  and expected/actual CRC detail, and does not get mislabeled as replacement
  `<sheetData>` payload input.
- The same regression verifies no mutation to queued workbook replacement,
  edit-plan notes, package-entry audits, payload audits, relationship audits,
  calc policy, manifest modes, or PackageEditor temp files.

Acceptance:
- Default preset:
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes.
- Minizip preset:
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes.

Forbidden:
- Do not make workbook catalog rename a full sheet rename implementation.
- Do not repair relationships, synthesize worksheet entries, migrate
  sharedStrings/styles, or expose public `PackageEditor`.
- Do not claim this completes the low-memory large-worksheet transformer.

## P8.198 - Contextualize by-name planned staged-chunk read failures

Status: done.

Goal: prove the C5 by-name helpers keep planned workbook sheet-name context when
the current worksheet input is an already queued staged worksheet chunk set that
is later mutated before a follow-up transform.

Input:
- P8.196 / P8.197 covered by-name source-entry read failures and direct planned
  staged-chunk read failures separately.
- The remaining gap was the combined path: planned workbook catalog rename,
  queued staged worksheet chunks, then a by-name follow-up transform that fails
  while consuming those planned chunks.

Output:
- `current_worksheet_input_source_description()` now includes the planned
  staged-chunk count next to the staged worksheet entry, owning worksheet part,
  and ZIP entry context.
- C5 regression now covers planned workbook catalog rename to `"Renamed"`,
  a queued staged worksheet chunk set, then
  `replace_worksheet_cells_by_name("Renamed", ...)` failing because the
  file-backed planned chunk size changed. The error keeps by-name sheet context,
  resolved worksheet part, cell-replacement analysis boundary, planned-chunk
  source with chunk count, expected/actual byte detail, chunk index, and
  file-backed path.
- C5 regression now covers the same planned-name / planned-chunk setup for
  `replace_worksheet_sheet_data_from_chunk_source_by_name("Renamed", ...)`
  failing on same-size CRC mutation. The error keeps the sheetData current-input
  output boundary, by-name context, chunk count, expected/actual CRC detail,
  chunk index, and file-backed path, without being mislabeled as replacement
  `<sheetData>` payload input.
- Both regressions verify no mutation to queued workbook / worksheet plans,
  notes, package-entry audits, removed package-entry audits, worksheet payload
  audits, worksheet relationship audits, calc policy, calcChain policy, or
  PackageEditor temp files.

Acceptance:
- Default preset:
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes.
- Minizip preset:
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes.

Forbidden:
- Do not turn planned workbook catalog rename into full sheet rename semantics.
- Do not repair relationships, migrate sharedStrings/styles, or expose public
  `PackageEditor`.
- Do not claim this completes low-memory large-file editing or streamed
  replacement-cell payloads.

## P8.199 - Expose planned staged-chunk composition in current-input diagnostics

Status: done.

Goal: make planned staged worksheet input diagnostics show whether the current
input is still file-backed instead of only reporting a total chunk count.

Input:
- P8.198 added planned staged-chunk count to current worksheet input source
  diagnostics.
- For C5 file-backed rewrite work, the more useful audit signal is the
  memory/file-backed composition of the planned chunk set.

Output:
- `current_worksheet_input_source_description()` now reports planned chunk
  composition as `N chunks; memory=M, file=F` and appends `unknown=U` if a test
  or invalid internal state uses an unsupported chunk kind.
- C5 by-name planned-name regression now asserts that both cell replacement
  size-mutation failures and sheetData CRC-mutation failures include
  `3 chunks; memory=2, file=1`, preserving by-name context, resolved worksheet
  part, current-input boundary, expected/actual detail, chunk index/path, and
  no-state-pollution behavior.

Acceptance:
- Default preset:
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes.
- Minizip preset:
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes.

Forbidden:
- Do not infer memory usage, streaming completion, or file-backed end-to-end
  guarantees from the diagnostic string alone.
- Do not expose public `PackageEditor` or change public API.

## P8.200 - Include planned staged-chunk expected bytes in current-input diagnostics

Status: done.

Goal: make C5 planned staged worksheet input diagnostics expose the recorded
byte-size contract for the whole staged chunk set, not just the chunk count and
memory/file composition.

Input:
- P8.199 added `memory=M, file=F` to planned staged-chunk current-input
  diagnostics.
- The lower-level reader already reports per-chunk expected/actual size or CRC,
  but the source description did not summarize the total recorded expected-size
  metadata for the current planned worksheet input.

Output:
- `planned_chunk_composition_description()` now appends `expected_bytes=N`,
  summing the `expected_size` metadata already recorded on staged
  `PackageEntryChunk` objects.
- If invalid internal test state has chunks without recorded expected sizes, the
  same description appends `chunks_without_expected_size=N`; overflow is
  reported as `expected_bytes=overflow`.
- No filesystem stat/read is added to build this description. The value comes
  only from recorded staged chunk metadata.
- C5 direct planned staged-chunk cell replacement size/CRC/missing-file failures
  now assert the summarized `expected_bytes`.
- The combined by-name planned workbook rename + planned staged-chunk failure
  regression now asserts the full source description:
  `3 chunks; memory=2, file=1, expected_bytes=N`.

Acceptance:
- Default preset:
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes.
- Minizip preset:
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes.

Forbidden:
- Do not treat `expected_bytes` as a runtime memory-footprint metric.
- Do not add package streaming, public editing API, relationship repair, or
  staged chunk repair.
- Do not claim this completes C5 large-file low-memory editing.

## P8.201 - Report current-input reader progress on read failures

Status: done.

Goal: make C5 current worksheet input failures identify how far the wrapper
reader progressed before a source-entry or planned staged-chunk read failed.

Input:
- P8.193-P8.200 already name the concrete current worksheet input source and
  staged chunk metadata.
- When a follow-up transform failed after some chunks were successfully emitted,
  the upper-level current-input wrapper did not report that progress.

Output:
- `CurrentWorksheetInputChunkReader` now tracks successful current-input chunks
  and bytes emitted through the wrapper.
- Read failures append `after emitting N current-input chunk(s) and B bytes`
  before the lower-level ZIP/staged-chunk diagnostic.
- The counter is updated only after a chunk is successfully returned to the
  caller. It does not pre-read, rewind, stat, or materialize worksheet XML.
- C5 planned staged-chunk size/missing-file failures assert progress after the
  first memory prefix chunk.
- C5 planned staged-chunk CRC failures assert progress after the memory prefix
  and file chunk have been emitted; the file chunk then fails at completion-time
  CRC validation before the suffix chunk is returned.
- C5 source-entry CRC failures assert progress after the source worksheet chunk
  has been emitted and then fails at source ZIP entry CRC validation.
- The by-name planned rename + planned staged-chunk regressions assert the same
  progress wording while preserving sheet-name and resolved worksheet-part
  context.

Acceptance:
- Default preset:
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes.
- Minizip preset:
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes.

Forbidden:
- Do not change chunk replay semantics or treat the progress counters as memory
  usage metrics.
- Do not add public diagnostics/API, package streaming, relationship repair, or
  staged chunk repair.

## P8.202 - Report last emitted current-input chunk size on read failures

Status: done.

Goal: make C5 current worksheet input failures identify the chunk boundary that
was last handed to the transformer before the next source-entry or planned
staged-chunk read failed.

Input:
- P8.201 reported total successful current-input chunks and bytes emitted.
- When a failure happened after several chunks, the diagnostic did not expose
  the last successfully emitted chunk size, making it harder to distinguish
  prefix/body/suffix boundaries in planned staged worksheet inputs.

Output:
- `CurrentWorksheetInputChunkReader` now records the size of the last
  successfully emitted current-input chunk.
- Read failures append `last chunk N bytes` after the existing emitted progress
  wording when at least one chunk has been returned to the caller.
- The counter is updated only after a chunk is successfully returned. It does
  not pre-read, rewind, stat, or materialize worksheet XML.
- C5 source-entry CRC failures assert that the last chunk size is the emitted
  source worksheet chunk size.
- C5 planned staged-chunk size, CRC, missing-file, and by-name planned-name
  regressions assert the last emitted chunk size for prefix/body boundaries.

Acceptance:
- Default preset:
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes.
- Minizip preset:
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes.

Forbidden:
- Do not treat `last chunk N bytes` as a memory-footprint metric.
- Do not add public diagnostics/API, package streaming, relationship repair, or
  staged chunk repair.
- Do not claim this completes C5 large-file low-memory editing.

- Direct `replace_worksheet_sheet_data()` staged chunk size/CRC mutation tests

- Direct `sheetData` planned staged chunk CRC-mutation tests now assert the
  completed emitted progress and last emitted body chunk size.
- This is a coverage-only C5 hardening slice; production behavior stays on the
  shared `CurrentWorksheetInputChunkReader` path.

Acceptance:
- Default preset:
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes.
- Minizip preset:
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes.

Forbidden:
- Do not add public diagnostics/API, package streaming, relationship repair, or
  staged chunk repair.
- Do not treat `last chunk N bytes` as memory-footprint evidence.
- Do not claim this completes C5 large-file low-memory editing.

## P8.204 - Extend sheetData source-entry last-chunk diagnostics coverage

- By-name `replace_worksheet_sheet_data_by_name()` source-entry read-failure
