## P8.205 - Report current worksheet input read attempts

Status: done.

Type: internal PackageEditor C5 diagnostic hardening + regression + docs; no
public API, no package format change, and no chunk replay semantic change.

Goal: make C5 current worksheet input failures identify the failing pull from
the wrapper reader, not just the already emitted chunk/byte progress. This keeps
source-entry and planned staged-chunk diagnostics actionable when the next
source read, size check, CRC check, or file open fails after one or more
successful chunks.

Input:
- P8.201 emitted current-input progress diagnostics.
- P8.202 `last chunk N bytes` diagnostics in `CurrentWorksheetInputChunkReader`.
- P8.203/P8.204 direct `sheetData` coverage for planned staged chunks and
  source-entry read failures.

Output:
- `CurrentWorksheetInputChunkReader` now tracks wrapper read attempts and
  appends `during current-input read attempt N` to read-failure diagnostics.
- C5 source-entry CRC/read failures now assert attempt 2 after the single source
  worksheet chunk was emitted and the next EOF/CRC validation pull failed.
- Direct planned staged-chunk size failures now assert attempt 2 for truncated
  file chunks and attempt 3 for overrun checks; planned staged-chunk CRC
  failures assert attempt 3 after the memory prefix and file-backed body chunk
  were emitted.
- Missing planned staged file failures assert attempt 2 after the memory prefix.
- Direct `sheetData` planned staged-chunk size/CRC failures and planned-name
  by-name `sheetData` source-entry failures assert the same attempt diagnostics.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes in the default NMake preset.
- Adjacent minizip C5 shard should pass when the opt-in preset is available.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose read-attempt counters as public API.
- Do not treat read attempt number as a memory-footprint metric.
- Do not claim this completes C5 large-file low-memory editing.

## P8.206 - Report PackageReader ZIP-entry chunk-source read progress

Status: done.

Type: internal PackageReader C5 diagnostic hardening + regression + docs; no
public API, no package format change, and no chunk replay semantic change.

Goal: make raw `PackageReader::entry_chunk_source()` failures as actionable as
the PackageEditor current-input wrapper by identifying the failing ZIP-entry
pull and how many chunks/bytes were emitted before the failure. This closes the
gap where source-entry CRC or IO failures named the ZIP entry but did not expose
the reader-side progress that produced the failure.

Input:
- P8.205 reports `CurrentWorksheetInputChunkReader` wrapper read attempts.
- `PackageReader::entry_chunk_source()` already backs direct source-entry C5
  current-input paths for stored and opt-in DEFLATE entries.
- Stored and DEFLATE chunk-source CRC failures already preserve entry name and
  expected/actual CRC, but raw reader diagnostics did not report pull progress.

Output:
- `with_zip_entry_chunk_source_context()` now tracks ZIP-entry chunk-source
  read attempts, emitted chunk count, emitted bytes, and last emitted chunk
  size.
- Stored source-entry chunk-source CRC failures now assert the lower-level
  `ZIP entry chunk-source read attempt N after emitting M chunks and B bytes`
  diagnostic, including `last chunk N bytes`.
- Opt-in minizip DEFLATE chunk-source CRC failures reuse the same lower-level
  progress diagnostic without assuming a fixed minizip/zlib chunk boundary.
- PackageEditor C5 source-entry failures continue to carry the upper
  current-input boundary while preserving the lower ZIP-entry chunk-source
  progress context.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_reader` passes in the default NMake preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_reader` passes in the minizip NMake preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes in the default NMake preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes in the minizip NMake preset.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose reader progress counters as public API.
- Do not treat chunk-source progress as a memory-footprint metric.
- Do not claim this completes C5 large-file low-memory editing.

## P8.207 - Report staged file-chunk reader progress

Status: done.

Type: internal PackageEditor C5 diagnostic hardening + regression + docs; no
public API, no package format change, and no chunk replay semantic change.

Goal: make planned staged file-chunk failures identify the lower file-reader
pull boundary, not just the outer current worksheet input pull. This keeps C5
planned-input diagnostics concrete when a previously validated file-backed
chunk is deleted, truncated, extended, or rewritten with same-size bytes before
a follow-up cell replacement or `sheetData` rewrite consumes it.

Input:
- P8.205 reports outer current-input read attempts.
- P8.206 reports lower `PackageReader` ZIP-entry chunk-source read progress.
- `PackageEntryChunkReader` already enforces recorded size/CRC contracts and
  names the staged chunk index/path, but file-backed replay failures did not
  report the per-file read attempt or bytes read before failure.

Output:
- `PackageEntryChunkReader` now tracks staged file-chunk read attempts and
  bytes read for the currently open file-backed chunk.
- Missing staged file, truncated file, extended file, and same-size CRC mutation
  failures now append staged file-chunk read progress.
- Existing C5 regressions for cell replacement and `sheetData` planned staged
  chunk failures assert the new lower progress diagnostic while preserving the
  outer current-input attempt/progress diagnostics and no-state-pollution checks.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes in the default NMake preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes in the minizip NMake preset.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose staged file-reader progress counters as public API.
- Do not treat staged file-reader progress as memory-footprint evidence.
- Do not claim this completes C5 large-file low-memory editing.

## P8.208 - Report reader materialization and extraction consumer progress

Status: done.

Type: internal PackageReader C5 diagnostic hardening + regression + docs; no
public API, no package format change, and no chunk-source semantic change.

Goal: make reader-side `read_entry()` materialization and
`extract_entry_to_file()` file extraction failures identify the consumer-layer
pull boundary, not just the lower ZIP-entry callback or the expected/actual
size contract. This keeps C5 source-entry diagnostics actionable when a raw
chunk source emits an empty chunk, ends early, over-produces bytes, or throws
while the materialization/extraction loop is consuming it.

Input:
- P8.206 reports lower `PackageReader::entry_chunk_source()` read
  attempt/progress for stored and opt-in DEFLATE source-entry failures.
- P8.207 reports planned staged file-chunk reader progress in PackageEditor.
- `read_entry_chunks_to_string()` and `extract_entry_chunks_to_file()` already
  enforced exact expected byte counts, but their size-contract diagnostics did
  not report how far the materialization/extraction consumer had progressed.

Output:
- `read_entry_chunks_to_string()` now tracks materialization read attempts,
  consumed chunk count, consumed bytes, and last consumed chunk size, and
  appends that progress to chunk-source callback and size-contract failures.
- `extract_entry_chunks_to_file()` now tracks file-extraction read attempts,
  consumed chunk count, consumed bytes, and last consumed chunk size before
  commit, and appends that progress to chunk-source callback, write, and
  size-contract failures.
- `fastxlsx.package_reader` regressions assert consumer progress for
  materialization and extraction short-read, over-production, and empty-chunk
  failures while preserving the existing expected/actual size diagnostics and
  extraction output preservation behavior.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_reader` passes in the default NMake preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_reader` passes in the minizip NMake preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes in the default NMake preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes in the minizip NMake preset.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose materialization/extraction progress counters as public API.
- Do not treat consumer progress as memory-footprint evidence.
- Do not claim this completes C5 large-file low-memory editing.

## P8.209 - Report staged package-entry chunk replay progress

Status: done.

Type: internal PackageEditor C5 diagnostic hardening + regression + docs; no
public API, no package format change, and no chunk replay semantic change.

Goal: make staged `PackageEntryChunkReader` failures identify the overall replay
pull boundary, not just the memory/file chunk detail. This keeps C5 planned
staged-chunk diagnostics actionable when replay fails before emitting anything,
after one or more prior chunks, or when an invalid internal chunk kind reaches
the replay layer.

Input:
- P8.207 reports lower file-backed chunk read progress.
- P8.208 reports reader materialization/extraction consumer progress.
- `PackageEntryChunkReader` already validated memory-backed size/CRC and
  file-backed size/CRC contracts, but stale memory chunk and unknown-kind
  failures did not report the staged replay attempt or emitted progress.

Output:
- `PackageEntryChunkReader` now tracks staged replay read attempts, emitted
  chunk count, emitted bytes, and last emitted chunk size, and appends that
  progress to replay-layer failures.
- Unknown internal chunk kinds are left for the replay boundary to report with
  staged replay progress, while production staged-chunk preflight still rejects
  invalid chunk descriptors before state mutation.
- File-backed staged chunk readers close any open file handle when destroyed,
  so early-abandoned staged replay does not leave PackageEditor-owned temporary
  worksheet XML files locked on Windows.
- `PackageEditor::save_as()` scopes materialized output-entry chunks so
  temporary source-copy files can be removed only after write-package consumers
  have released their file-backed chunk references.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` regressions cover stale memory size/CRC,
  unknown chunk kind, prior emitted chunk progress, and early file-backed reader
  destruction without temp-file leakage.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes in the default NMake preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.core` passes in the default NMake preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes in the minizip NMake preset.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose staged replay progress counters as public API.
- Do not treat staged replay progress as memory-footprint evidence.
- Do not claim this completes C5 large-file low-memory editing.

## P8.210 - Report staged package-entry chunk replay cursor

Status: done.

Type: internal PackageEditor C5 diagnostic hardening + regression + docs; no
public API, no package format change, and no chunk replay semantic change.

Goal: make staged `PackageEntryChunkReader` replay failures identify the
current staged chunk cursor as well as the overall replay progress. This keeps
multi-chunk C5 planned-input diagnostics precise when a later memory-backed
chunk fails its recorded size/CRC contract after earlier chunks were already
emitted.

Input:
- P8.209 reports replay read attempts, emitted chunk count, emitted bytes, and
  last emitted chunk size.
- Multi-chunk failures still required the caller to infer the current chunk
  position from the repeated `staged package-entry chunk N` detail.

Output:
- `PackageEntryChunkReader` replay errors now append
  `staged package-entry chunk index N of M` before the replay progress text.
- Memory-backed size/CRC contract failures preserve the cursor for the
  completed chunk that failed, not only the reader's next chunk index.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` regressions cover a three-memory-chunk replay
  where the third chunk fails after two prior chunks were emitted, plus the
  existing stale memory, empty-memory CRC, and unknown-kind paths.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes in the default NMake preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes in the minizip NMake preset.
- `git diff --check` passes for the touched C5 implementation, tests, and docs.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose staged replay cursor/progress counters as public API.
- Do not treat staged replay cursor diagnostics as memory-footprint evidence.
- Do not claim this completes C5 large-file low-memory editing.

## P8.211 - Report staged package-entry replay expected total

Status: done.

Type: internal PackageEditor C5 diagnostic hardening + regression + docs; no
public API, no package format change, and no chunk replay semantic change.

Goal: make staged `PackageEntryChunkReader` replay failures expose the recorded
expected total payload bytes for the full staged input. This keeps C5
planned-input diagnostics useful when emitted progress is lower than the whole
planned staged payload and the caller needs the total expected boundary without
reconstructing it from each chunk.

Input:
- P8.209 reports replay read attempts, emitted chunk count, emitted bytes, and
  last emitted chunk size.
- P8.210 reports the current staged chunk index and total chunk count.
- Planned staged chunks already carry expected-size metadata from preflight.

Output:
- `PackageEntryChunkReader` accumulates expected staged payload bytes from the
  recorded expected-size metadata used by replay.
- Replay errors now append `expected staged payload total N bytes` in the same
  progress section as emitted chunks and last-chunk size.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` regressions assert the expected total for stale
  memory chunk size failures, empty-memory CRC failures, unknown internal chunk
  kind failures, and a three-memory-chunk replay failure after two prior chunks.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes in the default NMake preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes in the minizip NMake preset.
- `git diff --check` passes for the touched C5 implementation, tests, and docs.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose staged replay expected-total counters as public API.
- Do not treat expected-total diagnostics as memory-footprint evidence.
- Do not claim this completes C5 large-file low-memory editing.

## P8.212 - Report current staged chunk expected bytes

Status: done.

Type: internal PackageEditor C5 diagnostic hardening + regression + docs; no
public API, no package format change, and no chunk replay semantic change.

Goal: make staged `PackageEntryChunkReader` replay failures expose the failing
chunk's recorded expected byte count next to the replay cursor. This keeps C5
planned-input diagnostics concrete for both memory-backed and file-backed
chunks, without requiring callers to infer the current chunk size from total
expected bytes and prior emitted progress.

Input:
- P8.210 reports the current staged chunk index and total chunk count.
- P8.211 reports the full staged payload expected total.
- `PackageEntryChunkReader` already keeps a per-chunk expected-size vector for
  replay-time size/CRC validation.

  `staged package-entry chunk index N of M` cursor.
- Memory-backed size/CRC contract failures report the expected byte count for
  the chunk that failed, including empty memory chunks.
- Unknown internal chunk kind failures report `expected chunk 0 bytes`, matching
  the placeholder replay-boundary metadata used for invalid internal chunk
  descriptors.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` regressions assert current-chunk expected bytes
  for stale memory size, stale empty-memory CRC, unknown-kind, and three-chunk
  replay failures.

- `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes in the default NMake preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes in the minizip NMake preset.

## P8.213 - Report staged package-entry expected remaining bytes

Status: done.

Type: internal PackageEditor C5 diagnostic hardening + regression + docs; no
public API, no package format change, and no chunk replay semantic change.

Goal: make staged `PackageEntryChunkReader` replay failures expose the
remaining expected staged payload bytes after already-emitted chunks. This
keeps C5 planned-input diagnostics actionable when a later file-backed or
memory-backed chunk fails and the caller needs the unfinished payload boundary
without subtracting emitted bytes from the total by hand.

Input:
- P8.209 reports replay read attempts, emitted chunk/byte progress, and last
  emitted chunk size.
- P8.211 reports the full staged payload expected total.
- P8.212 reports the current failing chunk's expected byte count.
- Direct and by-name PackageEditor current-input wrappers already preserve the
  lower staged replay diagnostics inside their higher-level current-input
  boundary.

Output:
- `PackageEntryChunkReader` replay progress now appends
  `expected staged payload remaining N bytes`.
- The remaining count is based only on fully emitted chunks, so short-read,
  overrun, CRC, stale memory, and missing-file failures keep the failed pull in
  the remaining boundary instead of treating partial or failed reads as
  emitted output.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` regressions assert remaining expected bytes for
  direct staged replay failures plus direct cell-replacement and `sheetData`
  planned staged-chunk size/CRC/missing-file failures.
- Documentation records this as diagnostic hardening only; it does not change
  chunk replay semantics or claim full C5 completion.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes in the default NMake preset.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.c5` passes in the minizip NMake preset.
- `git diff --check` passes for the touched C5 implementation, tests, and docs.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose staged replay remaining-byte counters as public API.
- Do not treat remaining-byte diagnostics as memory-footprint evidence.
- Do not claim this completes C5 large-file low-memory editing.

## P8.214 - Preserve WorkbookEditor state after failed save_as

Status: done.

Type: public `WorkbookEditor` facade state-hygiene regression + Doxygen / docs;
no new public API, no package format change, and no new editing capability.

Goal: prove public `WorkbookEditor::save_as()` output-path failures are
pre-commit failures from the facade caller's point of view. A rejected output
path must not clear queued replacement / rename state, must not alter public
catalog or edit-summary diagnostics, and must not overwrite a prior
`last_edit_error()` from an edit operation.

Input:
- Current `WorkbookEditor::save_as()` already rejects writing over the source
  package and inherits PackageEditor path guards for missing parents and
  directory outputs.
- Public diagnostics had focused coverage for failed `replace_sheet_data()` and
  failed `rename_sheet()`, but not for failed `save_as()` preserving queued
  facade state.

Output:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` now queues a sheetData replacement and catalog
  rename, records a prior edit failure in `last_edit_error()`, then verifies
  `save_as()` failures for source-overwrite, empty output path, missing-parent
  output, non-directory-parent output, and existing-directory output leave public
  pending counts, replacement names,
  memory estimate, `worksheet_catalog()`, `pending_worksheet_edits()`, and
  `last_edit_error()` unchanged.
- The same regression verifies a later safe `save_as()` still writes the queued
  rename and sheetData replacement.
- The same regression also verifies a clean editor with no prior public edit
  failure keeps `last_edit_error()` empty after a rejected `save_as()`, then can
  still write the queued replacement to a safe output path.
- `WorkbookEditor::save_as()` Doxygen, README, `NEXT_STEPS.md`, and API design
  docs now state that rejected / failed `save_as()` does not clear queued public
  edits and does not update `last_edit_error()`.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes in the default preset.
- `git diff --check` passes for the touched README, public header, tests, and
  docs.

Forbidden:
- Do not add public `PackageEditor`, `WorksheetEditor`, random cell editing,
  semantic sheet rename, relationship repair, sharedStrings / styles migration,
  or transactional in-place save.
- Do not describe failed `save_as()` as committed output, automatic rollback, or
  full package transaction support.

## P8.215 - Cover WorkbookEditor blank replacement semantics

Status: done.

Type: public `WorkbookEditor::replace_sheet_data()` regression + Doxygen / docs;
no new public API, no random editing, and no package format change.

Goal: pin the current whole-`<sheetData>` replacement distinction between
explicit blank cells and missing cells so callers can reason about generated
worksheet XML without treating this facade as a random cell editor.

Scope:
- Add `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` coverage proving explicit `CellValue::blank()`
  writes an empty `<c>` cell and may carry a caller-supplied non-default
  `StyleId` as `s="N"`.
- Prove explicit default style on blank cells omits `s="0"`.
- Prove an empty input row vector advances row mapping but does not emit an
  explicit empty `<row>`.
- Prove pending replacement cell diagnostics count explicit blank cells and do
  not count empty row vectors.
- Preserve source `xl/styles.xml` bytes and keep style ids caller-owned: no
  validation, migration, merge, or existing-file style preservation claim.

Acceptance:
- `test_replace_sheet_data_distinguishes_blank_cells_from_missing_cells()`
  passes under `fastxlsx.workbook_editor`.
- Public header docs describe explicit blank output and empty-row mapping.
- API docs and NEXT_STEPS describe the boundary without claiming tombstone,
  erase semantics, random cell editing, or style preservation.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose `WorksheetEditor`, `set_cell()`, `erase_cell()`, tombstones, or
  random cell editing as part of this slice.
- Do not treat empty row vectors as explicit empty worksheet rows in the public
  Patch facade.
- Do not validate source workbook style ids or claim styles migration / merge.

## P8.216 - Cover WorkbookEditor no-op save_as roundtrip

Status: done.

Type: public `WorkbookEditor::save_as()` regression + Doxygen / docs; no new
public API and no package format change.

Goal: prove the current public Patch facade can write a safe output copy even
when the caller has queued no public edits, while keeping the behavior framed as
reader-backed roundtrip copy rather than in-place editing or public
`PackageEditor`.

Scope:
- Add `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` coverage opening a source workbook with
  document properties and immediately calling `save_as(output)`.
- Verify the fresh editor has no pending public edits, no pending replacement
  diagnostics, and no `last_edit_error()`.
- Verify no-op `save_as()` does not create pending diagnostics.
- Verify decompressed output package entries equal decompressed source package
  entries, then reopen the output through `WorkbookEditor`.

Acceptance:
- `test_noop_save_as_preserves_source_package_entries()` passes under
  `fastxlsx.workbook_editor`.
- Public header, README, API design docs, and NEXT_STEPS describe no-op
  `save_as()` as reader-backed roundtrip copy only.

Forbidden:
- Do not claim ZIP byte identity, source archive metadata preservation, atomic
  in-place save, public `PackageEditor`, or complete preservation API.
- Do not add or expose new public edit operations.

## P8.217 - Cover WorkbookEditor inspection last-error invariance

Status: done.

Type: public `WorkbookEditor` regression + docs; no new public API and no
package format change.

Goal: pin the current `last_edit_error()` contract for all public inspection and
pending-diagnostic methods, so caller-facing read-only queries cannot clear,
replace, or create diagnostics from failed public edits.

Scope:
- Extend `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` coverage after failed `replace_sheet_data()`
  and failed `rename_sheet()` calls.
- Exercise `worksheet_names()`, `has_worksheet()`,
  `source_worksheet_names()`, `has_source_worksheet()`,
  `has_pending_changes()`, `pending_change_count()`,
  `pending_replacement_cell_count()`,
  `pending_replacement_worksheet_names()`, `has_pending_replacement()`,
  `estimated_pending_replacement_memory_usage()`,
  `pending_worksheet_edits()`, and `worksheet_catalog()`.
- Verify each call preserves the exact previous `last_edit_error()` optional
  value.
- Update API design docs and NEXT_STEPS to describe the broader inspection /
  pending diagnostic boundary.

Acceptance:
- `test_last_edit_error_tracks_failed_public_edits()` passes under
  `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` with the expanded inspection coverage.
- Focused build and CTest continue to pass.

Forbidden:
- Do not add a new diagnostic channel, expose internal `EditPlan`, or turn
  inspection methods into mutating APIs.
- Do not change save output semantics, queued replacement state, rename state,
  or package preservation behavior in this slice.

## P8.218 - Cover WorkbookEditor no-op save_as followed by edits

Status: done.

Type: public `WorkbookEditor::save_as()` regression + docs; no new public API
and no package format change.

Goal: prove a no-op `save_as()` does not close, commit, or invalidate the public
Patch facade. A caller can still queue later public edits and save them to a
different output path.

Scope:
- Add `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` coverage that opens a source workbook, calls
  `save_as(noop_output)` with no queued edits, then queues `replace_sheet_data()`
  and `rename_sheet()`.
- Verify pending diagnostics, planned catalog inspection, and source catalog
  inspection remain usable after the no-op save.
- Verify a later `save_as(edited_output)` writes the queued replacement and
  rename.
- Verify the earlier no-op output remains a reader-backed copy of the source and
  is not mutated by later editor operations.
- Update API design docs and NEXT_STEPS to frame this as continued facade
  usability, not transaction history or automatic pending-state commit.

Acceptance:
- `test_noop_save_as_keeps_editor_usable_for_later_edits()` passes under
  `fastxlsx.workbook_editor`.
- Focused build and CTest continue to pass.

Forbidden:
- Do not change no-op `save_as()` into in-place save, transaction commit,
  automatic pending-state clear, undo history, or public `PackageEditor`.
- Do not add new public edit operations or expose internal package output plans.

## P8.219 - Cover WorkbookEditor successful save_as state invariance

Status: done.

Type: public `WorkbookEditor::save_as()` regression + Doxygen / docs; no new
public API and no package format change.

Goal: pin successful `save_as()` as a reusable output operation rather than a
commit / close / pending-state-clear operation. Queued public edit diagnostics
remain visible after a successful save so callers can save the same planned
state to another output path or continue editing.

Scope:
- Add `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` coverage that queues a whole-`<sheetData>`
  replacement and sheet-catalog rename, records a prior failed public edit in
  `last_edit_error()`, then calls `save_as(first_output)` successfully.
- Verify `pending_change_count()`, pending replacement cell/name/memory
  diagnostics, `last_edit_error()`, `worksheet_catalog()`, and
  `pending_worksheet_edits()` remain unchanged after the successful save.
- Verify the first output contains the queued replacement and rename.
- Verify a second `save_as(second_output)` without new edits produces the same
  decompressed package entries, proving the planned state was not consumed.
- Verify a later successful `replace_sheet_data()` after `save_as()` can still
  update the planned replacement and write a third output, while earlier output
  packages remain unchanged.
- Update Doxygen / README / API docs / NEXT_STEPS to describe successful
  `save_as()` as reusable output, not commit.

Acceptance:
- `test_successful_save_as_preserves_public_facade_state()` passes under
  `fastxlsx.workbook_editor`.
- Focused build and CTest continue to pass.

Forbidden:
- Do not clear pending diagnostics, introduce commit/close state, add undo /
  transaction history, or expose internal package output plans.
- Do not change package preservation behavior, source package bytes, or public
  edit operations in this slice.

  returned.
- `fastxlsx.unit` covers self-closing `<f/>` and shared-formula-style
  `<f t="shared" si="0"/>` source payloads.
- Internal loader docs and task docs record this as source materialization
  shape policy only.

- `fastxlsx.unit` passes.
- `git diff --check` passes for the touched test, implementation, header, and
  docs.

Forbidden:
- Do not add public `WorksheetEditor`, random cell editing, or public
  `PackageEditor`.

Status: done.

Type: internal `CellStore` worksheet loader regression + docs; no public API,

  row/cell context for `<is>` and `<r>` wrappers.
- `fastxlsx.unit` covers `<is><r><t>...</t></r></is>` and
  `<phoneticPr .../>` rejection.
- Internal loader docs and task docs record this as source materialization
  fail policy only.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.unit` passes.
- `fastxlsx.worksheet_event_reader` / `fastxlsx.worksheet_transformer` pass

  no audit notes, and original worksheet/calcChain/unknown entry bytes remain

  package root `officeDocument` relationships.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` now extends the package-backed
  `load_cell_store_from_workbook_sheet()` officeDocument matrix to cover
  incomplete percent escapes and decoded null bytes, in addition to the
  existing invalid percent escape case.
- The package-backed loader still wraps failures with the requested sheet name
  and preserves a separately opened `PackageEditor` as copy-original with no
  edit-plan, note, calc policy, manifest, or output byte pollution.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_reader` and `fastxlsx.package_editor` pass.
- Full default `ctest` passes.
- `git diff --check` passes for touched tests and docs.

Forbidden:
- Do not add public `WorksheetEditor`, random cell editing, public
  `PackageEditor`, stable low-level diagnostic objects, alternate workbook

  chunk-source handoff.

  output replacement cells do not gain `s` attributes, `xl/styles.xml` remains
  copy-original in the output plan, and output `xl/styles.xml` bytes are
  preserved.

  source-loaded payload.

cell type and inline-string shape failures that the direct worksheet XML loader
already rejects.

Output:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata` now runs a package-backed failure matrix
  for error cells (`t="e"`), date-like cells (`t="d"`), inline-string payloads
  in non-inline cells, and ordinary `<v>` payloads in `t="inlineStr"` cells.
- Each case verifies the error includes workbook sheet, worksheet part, ZIP
  entry, and the underlying loader diagnostic.
- Each case verifies `EditPlan`, notes, calcChain policy, workbook recalculation
  flag, worksheet/calcChain manifest modes, and saved output bytes stay
  copy-original.
- Docs record this as failure-before-edit-state hygiene only; it is not error
  cell loading, date cell loading, inline-string repair, schema validation, or
  public random cell editing.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata` passes.
- Full default `ctest` passes.
- `git diff --check` passes for touched tests and docs.

Forbidden:
- Do not coerce error/date-like cells, flatten mismatched inline-string payloads,
  or treat source shape failures as partially loadable `CellStore` input.
- Do not add public `WorksheetEditor`, random cell editing, public
  `PackageEditor`, sharedStrings migration/rebuild, style id migration,
  stylesheet merge, XML repair, or relationship repair/pruning.

## P8.282 - Pin package-backed CellStore semantic load and handoff smoke

Status: done.

Type: internal `CellStore` package-backed source materialization regression +
docs; no public API and no package format change.

Goal: extend the package-backed by-name source-loaded success path beyond the
single formula smoke by proving the loader materializes the same basic semantic
cell values as the direct worksheet XML loader, then hands them to the existing
by-name `sheetData` Patch helper.

Output:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata` now loads a real package worksheet
  containing numeric cells, boolean cells, inline strings with XML entity
  decoding / `xml:space`, formula cells with cached values, explicit blank
  cells, and zero numeric cells.
- The test verifies the returned `CellStore` semantic records and then replaces
  the same sheet through `cell_store_sheet_data_chunk_source()` plus
  `replace_worksheet_sheet_data_from_chunk_source_by_name()`.
- The output verifies numeric / boolean / inline text / formula / blank / zero
  serialization, proves cached formula values are not carried through the sparse
  semantic store, applies the existing default calcChain cleanup /
  fullCalcOnLoad path, and preserves unknown package bytes.
- Docs record this as an internal package-backed source materialization smoke
  only; it is not public random cell editing, formula evaluation, cached-value
  preservation, sharedStrings migration, style migration, or relationship repair.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.sheetdata` passes.
- Full default `ctest` passes.
- `git diff --check` passes for touched tests and docs.

Forbidden:
- Do not preserve cached formula values as `CellStore` payload, evaluate
  formulas, migrate sharedStrings/styles, or expand this into public
  `WorksheetEditor` / `PackageEditor` API.
- Do not change relationship/content-type repair semantics.

## P8.285 - Audit F2 public WorksheetEditor gate evidence

Status: done.

Type: F2 public API gate audit docs; no code, no public header, and no package
format change.

Goal: stop the F2 track from accumulating duplicate internal negative tests and
decide whether the current internal evidence is sufficient to move into public
API design work.

Verdict:
- The loader/materialization evidence is sufficient for API design drafting:
  source-loaded `CellStore` success, dependency/shape fail policy,
  load-time `CellStoreOptions`, returned-store option persistence, and package
  state hygiene now have focused coverage.
- The mutation semantics are sufficiently pinned for design drafting:
  `try_cell()` distinguishes missing from explicit blank, `erase_cell()` removes
  sparse records, and `CellValue::blank()` remains the explicit blank replacement
  cell.
- The save-as evidence is still a narrow internal handoff, not a public
  implementation pass: current coverage proves source-loaded edits can flow
  through by-name `sheetData` Patch and preserve unknown bytes / calc cleanup,
  but it does not prove complete source-backed random edit save-as.
- Therefore the next task should be a `WorksheetEditor` public API design spec
  / Doxygen wording draft. Do not add `WorksheetEditor` to `include/` yet.

Output:
- F2 overview now records that P8.285 moves the track from repeated internal
  guardrail hunting to public API design drafting only.
- `docs/API_DESIGN_AND_DOCUMENTATION.md` now carries a current gate audit block
  that separates design-ready evidence from implementation blockers.
- `docs/NEXT_STEPS.md` now points the next narrow step at design spec work and
  keeps code work limited to proven evidence gaps or test-shard budget work.

Acceptance:
- Documentation clearly answers whether F2 can proceed: yes for public API
  design, no for public header implementation.
- Documentation lists remaining blockers before implementation:
  workbook-level / worksheet-level options naming, handle lifetime, public
  diagnostics / recovery wording, and complete source-backed random edit
  save-as contract.
- `git diff --check` passes for touched docs.

Forbidden:
- Do not add public `WorksheetEditor`, random cell editing, public
  `PackageEditor`, sharedStrings/style migration, relationship repair, XML
  repair, formula evaluation, or calcChain rebuild.
- Do not claim current `CellStoreOptions::memory_budget_bytes` is exact process
  RSS or save-time package assembly peak memory.

## P8.286 - Draft first public WorksheetEditor API slice

Status: done.

Type: F2 public API design docs; no code, no public header, and no package
format change.

Goal: convert the P8.285 gate audit into a concrete first-slice
`WorksheetEditor` API design draft while keeping implementation blocked until
remaining contracts and tests are explicit.

Output:
- `docs/API_DESIGN_AND_DOCUMENTATION.md` now defines the first public slice as
  In-memory / existing-workbook small-file editing, not Streaming and not a
  low-memory large worksheet editor.
- The draft entry points are `WorkbookEditor::worksheet(name, options)` and
  `try_worksheet(name, options)`, both explicitly materializing only the
  requested current-planned worksheet.
- The draft separates current `WorkbookEditorOptions` replacement-payload
  limits from future `WorksheetEditorOptions` / `WorksheetLoadOptions`
  materialization limits, with draft `max_cells` and `memory_budget_bytes`
  names.
- The draft first methods are `name()`, `try_cell(row, column)`, `set_cell(row,
  column, CellValue)`, `erase_cell(row, column)`, `cell_count()`, and
  `estimated_memory_usage()`.
- The draft freezes current read/mutation semantics: missing is an empty
  `try_cell()` result, explicit blank is `CellValue::blank()`,
  `erase_cell()` removes the sparse record, and tombstones / style-preserving
  clear / row insertion / row deletion are deferred.
- The draft records blockers for implementation: style policy, source
  dependency failures, save-as handoff tests, operation mixing rules, and
  public failure-hygiene diagnostics.

Acceptance:
- The API design doc can answer what the first public `WorksheetEditor` slice
  would contain and what it must still reject or defer.
- The doc explicitly says no public header is added yet and does not claim
  random editing is ready.
- `git diff --check` passes for touched docs.

Forbidden:
- Do not add public `WorksheetEditor`, random cell editing implementation,
  public `PackageEditor`, sharedStrings/style migration, relationship repair,
  XML repair, formula evaluation, calcChain rebuild, row insertion/deletion, or
  metadata semantic editing.

## P8.287 - Add WorksheetEditor acceptance matrix and open decisions

Status: done.

Type: F2 public API design docs; no code, no public header, and no package
format change.

Goal: turn the P8.286 first-slice API draft into a reviewable acceptance matrix
so implementation does not start with unresolved lifecycle, style, diagnostic,
or save-as semantics.

Output:
- `docs/API_DESIGN_AND_DOCUMENTATION.md` now has a `WorksheetEditor`
  acceptance matrix covering API mode, materialization, options, handle
  lifetime, coordinate overloads, read API, mutation API, styles, source
  dependencies, save-as, and diagnostics.
- The matrix recommends row/column overloads as the first coordinate contract,
  `try_cell()` before `get_cell()`, rejecting non-default `StyleId` on
  `WorksheetEditor::set_cell()` until style registry / validation exists, and
  rejecting ambiguous `rename_sheet()` / whole-sheet `replace_sheet_data()`
  mixing on materialized sheets unless invalidation semantics are specified.
- The open-decision list records exact public option type names, handle
  ownership, `get_cell()` timing, materialization diagnostics, and whether the
  first save-as route is limited to whole-`sheetData` local rewrite.

Acceptance:
- A reviewer can tell which `WorksheetEditor` behaviors are recommended for the
  first public slice and which remain blocked.
- The doc makes public tests required before adding the header, especially for
  materialization limits, mutation no-state-pollution, operation mixing, and
  save-as handoff.
- `git diff --check` passes for touched docs.

Forbidden:
- Do not implement public `WorksheetEditor`, random cell editing, style registry
  integration, sharedStrings migration, relationship repair, row insertion /
  deletion, metadata semantic editing, formula evaluation, or calcChain rebuild.

## P8.289 - Define WorksheetEditor save-as public facade acceptance

Status: done.

Type: F2 public API design docs; no code, no public header, and no package
format change.

Goal: make the remaining `WorksheetEditor` implementation blocker explicit:
source-backed random cell edits are only public-ready after save-as behavior has
public facade acceptance tests and diagnostics.

Output:
- `docs/API_DESIGN_AND_DOCUMENTATION.md` now defines the future save-as
  acceptance design for `WorksheetEditor`.
- The design states that materialization without mutation should not create a
  pending edit unless a later implementation documents otherwise.
- Dirty tracking starts at successful `set_cell()` / `erase_cell()`; failed
  materialization, mutation preflight, limit checks, unsupported style policy,
  and rejected operation mixing must not create or clear pending edits.
- Required public tests are listed for new value writes, explicit blank
  replacement, erase projection, untouched source cell preservation,
  package-level copy-original preservation, calc policy, dimension policy,
  output path guards, operation mixing, and staged diagnostics.
- The design keeps relationship/range metadata as audit/fail/preserve only and
  explicitly forbids table range updates, defined-name updates, drawing/chart
  updates, hyperlink repair, relationship pruning, and orphan cleanup claims.

Acceptance:
- A future implementation task can derive public facade tests directly from the
  save-as acceptance design.
- The doc identifies dimension policy as a release-blocking decision rather
  than an accidental helper detail.
- `git diff --check` passes for touched docs.

Forbidden:
- Do not implement public `WorksheetEditor`, random cell editing, relationship
  repair, range metadata recalculation, sharedStrings/style migration, formula
  evaluation, calcChain rebuild, row insertion/deletion, metadata semantic
  editing, or public `PackageEditor`.

## P8.293 - Cover no-op WorkbookEditor save_as after failed edit

Status: done.

Type: public `WorkbookEditor::save_as()` regression + docs; no public API, no
package format change, and no product code change.

Goal: pin the last missing no-op save diagnostic combination: a prior failed
public edit can leave `last_edit_error()` set while the editor still has no
queued changes, and a no-op `save_as()` must preserve that diagnostic without
turning the editor into a committed/closed state.

Output:
- `tests/test_workbook_editor.cpp` adds
  `test_noop_save_as_preserves_failed_edit_diagnostic()`.
- The regression first triggers a missing-sheet `replace_sheet_data()` failure,
  verifies no pending edits were created, then runs no-op `save_as(output)`.
- It verifies the no-op output remains a reader-backed roundtrip copy of the
  source entries, `last_edit_error()` is unchanged, and pending diagnostics stay
  empty.
- It then queues a valid replacement after the no-op save, verifies the
  successful edit clears the failed-edit diagnostic, and saves to a second
  output path.
- `docs/NEXT_STEPS.md` records the strengthened no-op `save_as()` diagnostic
  contract.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passes.
- `git diff --check` passes for touched files.

Forbidden:
- Do not change `save_as()` into a commit/close operation.
- Do not make failed `save_as()` or no-op `save_as()` update
  `last_edit_error()`.
- Do not add public `WorksheetEditor`, random cell editing, public
  `PackageEditor`, transaction history, undo, or in-place save.

## P8.295 - Cover no-op WorkbookEditor save_as after failed rename

Status: done.

Type: public `WorkbookEditor::save_as()` regression + docs; no public API, no

Goal: complete the no-op save diagnostic matrix for the current public edit
facade by covering `rename_sheet()` failures, not only failed

Output:

  `test_noop_save_as_preserves_failed_rename_diagnostic()`.
- The regression triggers an invalid `rename_sheet("Data", "Bad/Name")`,
  verifies the editor has no pending changes and records `last_edit_error()`,
  then runs no-op `save_as(output)`.
- It verifies no-op output remains a reader-backed source-entry copy,
  `last_edit_error()` is unchanged, and pending edit summaries stay empty.
- It then queues a valid `rename_sheet("Data", "CleanName")`, verifies the
  successful edit clears the failed-rename diagnostic, and saves the renamed
  workbook.

  public editor work.

## P8.310 - Record WorksheetEditor implementation gate checklist

Status: done.

Type: review-only API design documentation; no public API, no product code, no
test membership change, and no package format change.

Goal: turn the current future `WorksheetEditor` draft into an implementation
gate checklist so the next public-header task cannot skip options naming,
handle lifetime, diagnostics, operation mixing, or save-as handoff decisions.

Output:
- `docs/API_DESIGN_AND_DOCUMENTATION.md` now records a P8.310 implementation
  gate status for the future first public `WorksheetEditor` slice.
- The gate requires a separate worksheet materialization options type instead
  of reusing current `WorkbookEditorOptions` replacement-payload guardrails.
- The gate requires explicit handle lifetime / invalidation behavior for
  rename, whole-`<sheetData>` replacement, move construction / assignment, and
  repeated materialization before any `WorksheetEditor` reference or handle is
  public.
- The gate keeps `try_cell(row, column)` as the preferred first read API and
  preserves current sparse-store mutation semantics:
  `set_cell()` overwrites, `erase_cell()` removes the record, and
  `CellValue::blank()` writes an explicit blank replacement cell.
- The gate requires materialized edits to persist only through
  `WorkbookEditor::save_as()` and forbids claiming sharedStrings migration,
  style migration, relationship repair, range metadata recalculation, formula
  evaluation, calcChain rebuild, or in-place save.
- `docs/NEXT_STEPS.md` and `docs/TASK_PLAN.md` record this as a design gate, not
  an implemented public header.

Acceptance:
- Public-header grep still finds no `WorksheetEditor`, `WorksheetEditorOptions`,
  `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`, `try_worksheet()`, public `get_cell()`,
  `set_cell()`, or `erase_cell()` facade under `include/fastxlsx`.
- `git diff --check` passes for touched docs and tooling.

Forbidden:
- Do not add public `WorksheetEditor`, random cell editing, public
  `PackageEditor`, relationship repair, sharedStrings/style migration, formula
  evaluation, calcChain rebuild, transaction history, undo, or in-place save.
- Do not describe the gate checklist as implementation completion.

## P8.311 - Resolve WorksheetEditor materialization diagnostic surface

Status: done.

Type: review-only API design documentation; no public API, no product code, no
test membership change, and no package format change.

Goal: remove one blocker from the future `WorksheetEditor` first-slice gate by
deciding that materialization failures are load/lookup failures, not queued edit
failures, and therefore must not reuse current `WorkbookEditor::last_edit_error()`.

Output:
- `docs/API_DESIGN_AND_DOCUMENTATION.md` now records the P8.311 diagnostic
  decision.
- Current `last_edit_error()` stays scoped to failed public edit operations that
  try to queue workbook changes, currently `replace_sheet_data()` and
  `rename_sheet()`.
- Future `worksheet(name, options)` materialization failures should throw
  `FastXlsxError` with sheet name, phase, and source package context where
  available. Failure must occur before a caller-visible `WorksheetEditor` handle
  is returned and before queued edit state changes.
- Future `try_worksheet(name, options)`, if included, may return empty only for
  missing worksheet names. Unsupported source content, malformed XML, guardrail
  overflow, ZIP read failures, and dependency-policy failures still throw.
- The first slice should not add `last_materialization_error()`; if persistent
  materialization diagnostics become necessary, that requires a separate task
  and tests.
- `docs/NEXT_STEPS.md` and `docs/TASK_PLAN.md` record this as a design decision,
  not an implemented public API.

Acceptance:
- Public-header grep still finds no `WorksheetEditor`, `WorksheetEditorOptions`,
  `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`, `try_worksheet()`, public `get_cell()`,
  `set_cell()`, or `erase_cell()` facade under `include/fastxlsx`.
- `git diff --check` passes for touched docs and tooling.

Forbidden:
- Do not add public `WorksheetEditor`, random cell editing, public
  `PackageEditor`, relationship repair, sharedStrings/style migration, formula
  evaluation, calcChain rebuild, transaction history, undo, or in-place save.
- Do not widen current `last_edit_error()` beyond failed queued public edits.

## P8.312 - Freeze WorksheetEditor options naming and passing location

Status: done.

Type: review-only API design documentation; no public API, no product code, no
test membership change, and no package format change.

Goal: remove the next implementation-gate blocker by choosing the future
worksheet materialization options name, fields, and call site before exposing
any `WorksheetEditor` header.

Output:
- `docs/API_DESIGN_AND_DOCUMENTATION.md` now records the P8.312 decision:
  the future first public slice should use `WorksheetEditorOptions`, not
  `WorksheetLoadOptions`.
- `WorksheetEditorOptions` is passed per
  `WorkbookEditor::worksheet(name, options)` /
  `WorkbookEditor::try_worksheet(name, options)` materialization call, not
  stored in `WorkbookEditor::open()`.
- The first fields remain `std::optional<std::size_t> max_cells` and
  `std::optional<std::size_t> memory_budget_bytes`; they guard both source
  materialization and later sparse-store growth from mutations.
- The decision keeps `WorksheetEditorOptions` separate from current
  `WorkbookEditorOptions`, whose `max_replacement_cells` and
  `replacement_memory_budget_bytes` only apply to
  `WorkbookEditor::replace_sheet_data()` replacement payloads.
- Per-call options preserve cheap `open()`, avoid implicit cell loading, and
  allow different sheets to use different materialization budgets.

Acceptance:
- Public-header grep still finds no `WorksheetEditor`, `WorksheetEditorOptions`,
  `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`, `try_worksheet()`, public `get_cell()`,
  `set_cell()`, or `erase_cell()` facade under `include/fastxlsx`.
- `git diff --check` passes for touched docs and tooling.

Forbidden:
- Do not add public `WorksheetEditor`, random cell editing, public
  `PackageEditor`, relationship repair, sharedStrings/style migration, formula
  evaluation, calcChain rebuild, transaction history, undo, or in-place save.
- Do not reinterpret current `WorkbookEditorOptions` as worksheet load or
  random-edit limits.
- Do not make `WorkbookEditor::open()` implicitly materialize worksheet cells.

## P8.313 - Freeze WorksheetEditor handle lifetime and operation mixing

Status: done.

Type: review-only API design documentation; no public API, no product code, no
test membership change, and no package format change.

Goal: remove the next implementation-gate blocker by choosing safe first-slice
ownership, reference lifetime, repeated materialization, and operation mixing
rules before exposing any `WorksheetEditor` header.

Output:
- `docs/API_DESIGN_AND_DOCUMENTATION.md` now records the P8.313 decision:
  `WorkbookEditor` owns materialized worksheet state and
  `worksheet(name, options)` returns a borrowed `WorksheetEditor&`.
- `try_worksheet(name, options)` should return an optional reference wrapper for
  that same owned state, returning empty only for missing sheet names per
  P8.311.
- Handles remain valid only while the owning `WorkbookEditor` is alive and
  unmoved. Moving or move-assigning the owner invalidates previously obtained
  references; callers must reacquire handles from the moved-to editor.
- Repeated materialization of the same current-planned sheet may return the
  existing handle only when requested options match the first materialization
  options. Different options fail before state changes.
- First-slice operation mixing is reject-first: materialized sheets cannot be
  renamed or whole-`<sheetData>` replaced, and sheets with queued rename or
  whole-sheet replacement cannot be materialized until a later task specifies
  migration semantics.

Acceptance:
- Public-header grep still finds no `WorksheetEditor`, `WorksheetEditorOptions`,
  `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`, `try_worksheet()`, public `get_cell()`,
  `set_cell()`, or `erase_cell()` facade under `include/fastxlsx`.
- `git diff --check` passes for touched docs and tooling.

Forbidden:
- Do not add public `WorksheetEditor`, random cell editing, public
  `PackageEditor`, relationship repair, sharedStrings/style migration, formula
  evaluation, calcChain rebuild, transaction history, undo, or in-place save.
- Do not introduce detached worksheet handles that can outlive the
  `WorkbookEditor` package edit session.
- Do not silently migrate materialized sparse stores across rename,
  whole-sheet replacement, move, or changed options.

## P8.314 - Freeze WorksheetEditor save-as handoff behavior

Status: done.

Type: review-only API design documentation; no public API, no product code, no
test membership change, and no package format change.

Goal: remove the save-as behavior design blocker by choosing the first public
slice persistence semantics and dimension requirement before exposing any
`WorksheetEditor` header.

Output:
- `docs/API_DESIGN_AND_DOCUMENTATION.md` now records the P8.314 decision:
  materializing a worksheet without mutation does not create pending public edit
  state, and no-op `save_as()` remains a reader-backed roundtrip copy.
- Successful `set_cell()` / `erase_cell()` marks the materialized worksheet
  dirty inside the owning `WorkbookEditor`; persistence still happens only via
  `WorkbookEditor::save_as()`.
- Public tests must prove set / explicit blank / erase projection, untouched
  source cell preservation, package-level copy-original preservation, and
  failure-before-state-change on path guards or projection failures.
- Edited worksheet output must refresh top-level worksheet `<dimension>` to
  match emitted cell extents before any public header is exposed.
- Other range-bearing metadata remains audit / fail / preserve only; the first
  slice must not claim table range updates, defined-name updates, drawing/chart
  updates, hyperlink repair, relationship pruning, or orphan cleanup.
- Formula cells still use recalculation request / calcChain cleanup policy; no
  formula evaluation or calcChain rebuild is introduced.

Acceptance:
- Public-header grep still finds no `WorksheetEditor`, `WorksheetEditorOptions`,
  `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`, `try_worksheet()`, public `get_cell()`,
  `set_cell()`, or `erase_cell()` facade under `include/fastxlsx`.
- `git diff --check` passes for touched docs and tooling.

Forbidden:
- Do not add public `WorksheetEditor`, random cell editing, public
  `PackageEditor`, relationship repair, sharedStrings/style migration, formula
  evaluation, calcChain rebuild, transaction history, undo, or in-place save.
- Do not ship stale worksheet `<dimension>` as accepted first-slice behavior.
- Do not claim recalculation of tables, defined names, drawings, charts,
  hyperlinks, validations, conditional formatting, or other range metadata.

## P8.315 - Add CellStore dimension projection helper

Status: done.

Type: internal implementation + focused tests + docs; no public API, no CMake
membership change, and no package format change.

Goal: provide the first implementation evidence for the P8.314 dimension
refresh requirement without changing current Patch facade
`replace_sheet_data()` semantics.

Output:
- Added internal `cell_store_dimension_reference(const CellStore&)`.
- Empty stores return `A1`.
- Non-empty stores return the min/max rectangular extent of active sparse
  records, including explicit blank records.
- Focused `fastxlsx.unit` coverage now checks empty stores, sparse extents,
  erase-driven shrink, explicit blank extent growth, and source-loaded
  `CellStore` mutation before dimension projection.
- The helper is a building block for future in-memory save-as dimension refresh;
  it does not modify worksheet XML by itself.

Acceptance:
- `git diff --check` passes for touched files.
- Source-level public-header gate still finds no public `WorksheetEditor`,
  `WorksheetEditorOptions`, `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`, `try_worksheet()`,
  public `get_cell()`, `set_cell()`, or `erase_cell()` facade under
  `include/fastxlsx`.
- Focused build/test should be run from a VS2026 Developer Prompt with:
  `cmake --build --preset windows-nmake-release --target fastxlsx_tests` and
  `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R fastxlsx.unit`.

Validation note:
- In the current non-developer PowerShell, `nmake` is not on `PATH`, so local
  build execution cannot prove compilation here. The code path is still covered
  by focused source inspection and must be compiled in the configured VS2026
  environment before release.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose this helper as public API.
- Do not reinterpret it as full `WorksheetEditor` save-as support.
- Do not change current public `WorkbookEditor::replace_sheet_data()` to refresh
  wrapper dimension metadata as a side effect of this helper.
- Do not claim recalculation of tables, defined names, drawings, charts,
  hyperlinks, validations, conditional formatting, or other range metadata.

## P8.316 - Add internal CellStore worksheet projection chunk source

Status: done.

Type: internal implementation + focused tests + docs; no public API, no CMake
membership change, and no current Patch facade behavior change.

Goal: connect the P8.315 dimension projection helper to a dedicated
in-memory save-as handoff boundary without exposing `WorksheetEditor` or
changing whole-`<sheetData>` Patch preservation semantics.

Output:
- Added internal `cell_store_worksheet_chunk_source(const CellStore&)`.
- The helper emits a minimal full worksheet XML chunk stream: XML declaration,
  `<worksheet>` root, refreshed top-level `<dimension>`, existing chunked
  `<sheetData>` payload, and closing worksheet tag.
- Empty stores project as `<dimension ref="A1"/>` plus empty `<sheetData>`.
- Sparse stores project the dimension from active emitted records, including
  erase-driven shrink and explicit blank extent records.
- Existing style id attributes remain serialized in emitted cells, but the
  helper does not merge styles or validate that the destination workbook owns
  those ids.
- Internal package-editor smoke now feeds the full worksheet chunk source
  through by-name worksheet replacement and verifies staged `StreamRewrite`,
  refreshed `<dimension>`, calcChain cleanup / fullCalcOnLoad, and unknown-byte
  preservation.

Acceptance:
- `git diff --check` passes for touched files.
- `fastxlsx.unit` coverage checks empty worksheet projection, refreshed
  dimension in the full worksheet projection, chunked handoff shape, absence of
  sharedStrings migration, style id attribute preservation, explicit blank
  extent records, and a style-free projection roundtrip through the internal
  worksheet loader.
- `fastxlsx.package_editor` cellstore shard coverage checks the same helper as a
  full worksheet replacement source rather than only standalone `<sheetData>`.
- Source-level public-header gate still finds no public `WorksheetEditor`,
  `WorksheetEditorOptions`, `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`, `try_worksheet()`,
  public `get_cell()`, `set_cell()`, or `erase_cell()` facade under
  `include/fastxlsx`.
- Focused build/test should be run from a VS2026 Developer Prompt with:
  `cmake --build --preset windows-nmake-release --target fastxlsx_tests` and
  `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R fastxlsx.unit`.

Validation note:
- In the current non-developer PowerShell, `nmake` is not on `PATH`, so local
  build execution may still be blocked here. The code path must be compiled in
  the configured VS2026 environment before release.

Forbidden:
- Do not expose this helper as public API.
- Do not reinterpret it as public `WorksheetEditor`, random cell editing,
  public `PackageEditor`, or complete save-as support.
- Do not change current `WorkbookEditor::replace_sheet_data()` /
  `replace_worksheet_sheet_data*()` behavior to refresh preserved wrapper
  dimension metadata as a side effect of this helper.
- Do not claim sharedStrings migration, style merge, relationship repair,
  formula evaluation, calcChain rebuild, or recalculation of tables, defined
  names, drawings, charts, hyperlinks, validations, conditional formatting, or
  other range metadata.

## P8.317 - Cover WorkbookEditor rename-back public diagnostics

Status: done.

Type: public-facade regression test + public Doxygen/docs; no new public API
and no internal Patch API expansion.

Goal: close one focused operation-mixing guardrail before adding any
`WorksheetEditor` header by proving current `WorkbookEditor` planned-catalog
diagnostics stay reversible when a queued rename is changed back to the source
sheet name.

Output:
- Added `test_rename_back_to_source_name_restores_public_diagnostics()` to
  `tests/test_workbook_editor.cpp`.
- Updated `include/fastxlsx/workbook_editor.hpp` Doxygen to describe
  rename-back diagnostic migration without broadening the facade.
- The regression queues `replace_sheet_data("Data", ...)`, renames `Data` to a
  temporary planned name, then renames it back to `Data`.
- It verifies `worksheet_names()` / `has_worksheet()` return to the source name,
  `pending_replacement_worksheet_names()` and `has_pending_replacement()` migrate
  back to that restored planned name, and `worksheet_catalog()` /
  `pending_worksheet_edits()` clear the public `renamed` flag while keeping the
  queued replacement diagnostics.
- The saved package contains the restored catalog name and final replacement
  payload, and does not leak the transient planned name.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` covers rename-back public facade state hygiene.
- `git diff --check` passes for touched files.
- Source-level public-header gate still finds no public `WorksheetEditor`,
  `WorksheetEditorOptions`, `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`, `try_worksheet()`,
  public `get_cell()`, `set_cell()`, or `erase_cell()` facade under
  `include/fastxlsx`.
- Focused build/test should be run from a VS2026 Developer Prompt with:
  `cmake --build --preset windows-nmake-release --target fastxlsx_workbook_editor_tests` and
  `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R fastxlsx.workbook_editor`.

Validation note:
- Verified locally from VS2026 `vcvarsall.bat x64` environment with:
  `cmake --build --preset windows-nmake-release --target fastxlsx_workbook_editor_tests`
  and `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R fastxlsx.workbook_editor
  --output-on-failure --timeout 60`; `fastxlsx.workbook_editor` passed. A plain
  non-developer PowerShell may still fail before `vcvarsall.bat` because `nmake`
  is not on `PATH`.

Forbidden:
- Do not reinterpret this as semantic sheet rename support; definedNames,
  formulas, tables, drawings, charts, hyperlinks, relationship targets,
  sharedStrings, styles, and calcChain remain unsynchronized.
- Do not expose `WorksheetEditor`, random cell editing, public `PackageEditor`,
  relationship repair/pruning, sharedStrings migration, style merge, formula
  evaluation, or calcChain rebuild.

## P8.320 - Close public wording gate for current WorkbookEditor / future WorksheetEditor boundary

making README, API design docs, task plan, and next-step docs agree on what is
currently public and what must still wait behind the F2 implementation gate.

- `docs/API_DESIGN_AND_DOCUMENTATION.md` now records the P8.320 public wording
  gate: current `WorkbookEditor` wording is the implemented Patch facade only,
  and future `WorksheetEditor` / random-edit names remain design-only until an
  explicit public-header task opens them.

  complete" list: `WorksheetEditorOptions`, `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()` /
  `try_worksheet()`, `get_cell()` / `set_cell()` / `erase_cell()` must stay out
  of public headers until operation-mixing guardrails, dimension refresh, and
  save-as persistence tests land together.
- `docs/NEXT_STEPS.md` and `docs/TASK_PLAN.md` now move the next default F2
  candidate from wording cleanup to concrete operation-mixing guardrail evidence
  or a deliberately tested public-header implementation task.
- `docs/TASK_BREAKDOWN.md` now records this gate so future work does not treat
  repeated wording-only edits as the main blocker.

Acceptance:

- Source-level public-header gate still finds no public `WorksheetEditor`,
  `WorksheetEditorOptions`, `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`, `try_worksheet()`,
  public `get_cell()`, `set_cell()`, or `erase_cell()` facade under
  `include/fastxlsx`.
- No build is required for this slice because no C++ sources, public headers,
  CMake files, or tests are changed.

```powershell
rg -n "class WorksheetEditor|struct WorksheetEditor|WorksheetEditorOptions|WorkbookEditor::worksheet\(|try_worksheet\(|get_cell\(|set_cell\(|erase_cell\(" include\fastxlsx -g "*.hpp" -g "!include/fastxlsx/detail/**"
```

## P8.321 - Cover source-loaded CellStore planned-name handoff after queued rename

Status: done.

Type: internal regression test + docs; no public API, no public header, no CMake
membership change, and no package format expansion.

Goal: add concrete post-wording operation-mixing evidence without exposing
`WorksheetEditor`: when a source-loaded `CellStore` is handed to the existing
by-name `<sheetData>` Patch helper after a queued sheet-catalog rename, the
handoff must follow the current planned catalog name.

Output:
- Added
  `test_package_editor_source_loaded_cell_store_uses_planned_name_after_rename()`
  to `tests/test_package_editor.cpp`.
- The regression loads source `Sheet1` into internal `CellStore`, mutates the
  sparse store, queues `PackageEditor::rename_sheet_catalog_entry("Sheet1",
  "Loaded Data")`, then attempts the CellStore `<sheetData>` handoff by the old
  source name.
- The old-name handoff fails against the planned workbook catalog and preserves
  the queued workbook rename, worksheet copy-original state, calc policy, and
  edit-plan notes.
- A fresh handoff using the planned new name succeeds, rewrites the worksheet,
  removes stale `xl/calcChain.xml`, requests workbook recalculation, preserves
  unknown bytes, and leaves the output sheet catalog under `Loaded Data`.
- This is internal `PackageEditor` / `CellStore` evidence only. It does not
  create a public `WorksheetEditor`, borrowed handle, random cell editing API, or
  public materialized-worksheet operation-mixing contract.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.cellstore` covers source-loaded CellStore handoff
  across queued sheet rename.
- `git diff --check` passes for touched files.
- Source-level public-header gate still finds no public `WorksheetEditor`,
  `WorksheetEditorOptions`, `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`, `try_worksheet()`,
  public `get_cell()`, `set_cell()`, or `erase_cell()` facade under
  `include/fastxlsx`.

Validation note:
- Verified locally from VS2026 `vcvarsall.bat x64` environment with:
  `cmake --build --preset windows-nmake-release --target fastxlsx_package_editor_tests`
  and `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R fastxlsx.package_editor.cellstore
  --output-on-failure --timeout 60`; `fastxlsx.package_editor.cellstore` passed.

Forbidden:
- Do not reinterpret this as public `WorksheetEditor` readiness.
- Do not claim materialized worksheet handles can be safely renamed in public API
  without explicit handle lifetime / invalidation tests.
- Do not claim random cell editing, sharedStrings/style migration, semantic
  sheet rename, relationship repair, formula evaluation, calcChain rebuild, or
  public `PackageEditor`.

## P8.324 - Cover source-loaded CellStore sheetData patch after queued worksheet replacement

Status: done.

Type: internal regression test + docs; no public API, no public header, no CMake
membership change, and no package format expansion.

Goal: prove the future materialized worksheet save-as path can use the current
planned worksheet payload when a source-loaded `CellStore` is handed off after a
queued whole-worksheet replacement, instead of falling back to source worksheet
bytes or resurrecting source-only data.

Output:
- Added
  `test_package_editor_source_loaded_cell_store_patches_queued_worksheet_replacement()`.
- The regression loads source `Sheet1` into internal `CellStore`, erases the
  source formula, adds new text and explicit blank records, queues a whole
  worksheet replacement with wrapper metadata, then applies the existing
  by-name `<sheetData>` chunk-source helper from the `CellStore`.
- The output preserves queued `sheetViews`, `autoFilter`, and `extLst`, replaces
  only the queued `<sheetData>` payload, removes stale calcChain, keeps
  `fullCalcOnLoad`, preserves unknown bytes, and does not resurrect the erased
  source formula or earlier queued rows.
- This is internal `PackageEditor` / `CellStore` operation-mixing evidence only.
  It does not expose public `WorksheetEditor`, define public mixing semantics, or
  implement relationship repair / range metadata recalculation.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.cellstore` covers source-loaded `CellStore`
  sheetData handoff after queued whole-worksheet replacement.
- `git diff --check` passes for touched files.
- Source-level public-header gate still finds no public `WorksheetEditor`,
  `WorksheetEditorOptions`, `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`, `try_worksheet()`,
  public `get_cell()`, `set_cell()`, or `erase_cell()` facade under
  `include/fastxlsx`.

Validation note:
- Verified locally from VS2026 `vcvarsall.bat x64` environment with:
  `cmake --build --preset windows-nmake-release --target fastxlsx_package_editor_tests`
  and `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R fastxlsx.package_editor.cellstore
  --output-on-failure --timeout 60`; `fastxlsx.package_editor.cellstore` passed.

Forbidden:

## P8.326 - Cover CellStore sheetData handoff after queued rename and worksheet replacement

Status: done.

workbook catalog before consuming a source-loaded `CellStore` chunk source.

- Added
  `test_package_editor_source_loaded_cell_store_uses_planned_name_after_queued_rename_and_worksheet()`.
- The regression loads source `Sheet1` into internal `CellStore`, queues
  `Sheet1 -> Renamed Queued`, queues a whole worksheet replacement by the
  planned name, then attempts a `CellStore` `<sheetData>` handoff by the old
  source name.
- The old-name failure preserves the queued rename, queued worksheet
  `StreamRewrite`, calcChain removal, `fullCalcOnLoad`, notes, and manifest
  state, and consumes zero prepared `CellStore` chunks.
- The planned-name handoff then succeeds, replaces `<sheetData>` inside the
  queued worksheet wrapper, preserves the renamed workbook catalog, omits
  stale calcChain, and preserves unknown bytes.
- This is internal `PackageEditor` / `CellStore` operation-mixing evidence only.
  It does not expose public `WorksheetEditor`, define public retry semantics, or
  implement relationship repair / range metadata recalculation.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.cellstore` covers source-loaded `CellStore`
  sheetData handoff after queued rename plus queued whole-worksheet replacement.
- `git diff --check` passes for touched files.
- Source-level public-header gate still finds no public `WorksheetEditor`,

  truncate save-time copy-original temp files before package writing.
- The failure preserves edit-plan size, notes, calc policy, worksheet
  `StreamRewrite`, staged replacement chunks, existing output bytes, and
  source-copy / output-sibling temporary-file cleanup.
- A subsequent safe `save_as(output)` still succeeds and preserves the P8.327
  output semantics.
- This is internal save-time failure hygiene evidence only. It does not expose
  temp paths or hooks, add in-place save, transaction semantics, public
  `WorksheetEditor`, public retry semantics, or public `PackageEditor`.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.package_editor.cellstore` covers source-copy temp-size failure after
  the combined staged full worksheet projection state.
- `git diff --check` passes for touched files.
- Source-level public-header gate still finds no public `WorksheetEditor`,
  `WorksheetEditorOptions`, `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`, `try_worksheet()`,
  public `get_cell()`, `set_cell()`, or `erase_cell()` facade under
  `include/fastxlsx`.

Validation note:
- Verified locally from VS2026 `vcvarsall.bat x64` environment with:
  `cmake --build --preset windows-nmake-release --target
  fastxlsx_package_editor_tests` and `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R
  fastxlsx.package_editor.cellstore --output-on-failure --timeout 60`;
  `fastxlsx.package_editor.cellstore` passed.

  failure hygiene, source-copy temp hygiene, planned workbook removal, malformed
  planned catalog XML, namespace-filtered id failures, unqualified id failures,
  and unregistered planned worksheet targets are now covered for source-loaded
  `CellStore` chunk handoff.
- Froze the default next step as a public-header implementation task plan for
  the first `WorksheetEditor` slice, not another same-family internal negative

  option matching, operation-mixing rejection, dirty set/blank/erase projection,

  diagnostics.
- This refresh is not public `WorksheetEditor` readiness by itself. It does not
  add `WorksheetEditor`, `WorksheetEditorOptions`,

  `set_cell()`, or `erase_cell()`; it also does not add sharedStrings/style

  `TASK_PLAN.md` agree that additional same-family planned-catalog negative
  tests are no longer the default next action after P8.343.
- The docs point the next implementation lane at the first public
  `WorksheetEditor` slice task and its required public tests, while keeping the
  public-header gate closed.
- Source-level public-header gate still finds no public `WorksheetEditor`,
  `WorksheetEditorOptions`, `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`, `try_worksheet()`,
  public `get_cell()`, `set_cell()`, or `erase_cell()` facade under

## P8.346 - Add internal materialized worksheet session foundation

Status: done.

Type: internal implementation + unit test + docs; no public API, no public
header, no CMake membership change, and no package format expansion.

Goal: land the first private state holder needed by the future
`WorkbookEditor`-owned `WorksheetEditor` path, without exposing public random
cell editing.

Output:
- Added `include/fastxlsx/detail/materialized_worksheet_session.hpp` with
  internal `detail::MaterializedWorksheetSession`.
- The session owns a current planned sheet name, a `CellStore`, access to the
  store's `CellStoreOptions`, an `options_match()` helper for repeated
  materialization preflight, and a dirty flag.
- `set_cell()` delegates to `CellStore::set_cell()` and marks dirty only after
  success. `erase_cell()` delegates to `CellStore::erase_cell()` and marks dirty
  only when an existing sparse record was removed; erasing a missing cell is a
  clean no-op.
- Added `test_internal_materialized_worksheet_session()` to `fastxlsx.unit`.
  The regression covers loaded-clean state, planned name retention, option
  matching / mismatch, set / erase dirty transitions, missing-cell erase no-op,
  and failure-before-dirty for coordinate and max-cell guardrail failures.
- This is not a public `WorksheetEditor`, not public `WorksheetEditorOptions`,
  not `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()` / `try_worksheet()`, not public
  `get_cell()` / `set_cell()` / `erase_cell()`, and not a save-as handoff.

Acceptance:
- `fastxlsx.unit` covers internal materialized session dirty / option /
  guardrail semantics.
- `git diff --check` passes for touched files.
- Source-level public-header gate still finds no public `WorksheetEditor`,
  `WorksheetEditorOptions`, `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`, `try_worksheet()`,
  public `get_cell()`, `set_cell()`, or `erase_cell()` facade under
  `include/fastxlsx` outside `detail`.

Validation note:
- Verified locally from the VS2026 `vcvarsall.bat x64` environment with:
  `cmake --build --preset windows-nmake-release --target fastxlsx_tests` and
  `ctest --preset windows-nmake-release -R fastxlsx.unit --output-on-failure
  --timeout 60`; `fastxlsx.unit` passed.

## P8.349 - Add internal dirty materialized worksheet projection enumeration

  `MaterializedWorksheetSessionRegistry::dirty_worksheet_chunk_sources()`.
- Each projection carries a planned sheet name and a full worksheet chunk
  callback delegated from the dirty session's current sparse store.

  callers must keep the registry alive and avoid mutating those sessions while
  consuming the callbacks.

  clean sessions are skipped, dirty sessions are enumerated by planned-name
  registry order, and each projection emits refreshed dimension plus sparse
  payload.
- This is not `WorkbookEditor::save_as()` wiring, not a package edit queue, not
  public `WorksheetEditor`, and not relationship/style/sharedStrings migration.

Acceptance:

- `git diff --check` passes for touched files.
- Source-level public-header gate still finds no public `WorksheetEditor`,
  `WorksheetEditorOptions`, `WorkbookEditor::worksheet()`, `try_worksheet()`,
  public `get_cell()`, `set_cell()`, or `erase_cell()` facade under

  materialization preserving dirty state without reading a missing source sheet,
